name: gitlab_access_request_approve_group
description:
Approve a group access request. Optionally set the access level (10=Guest, 20=Reporter, 30=Developer, 40=Maintainer).

See also: gitlab_access_request_list_group, gitlab_group_member_add

Returns: JSON with the approved access request details.

user_id.description: User ID of the access requester,required

name: gitlab_access_request_approve_project
description:
Approve a project access request. Optionally set the access level (10=Guest, 20=Reporter, 30=Developer, 40=Maintainer).

See also: gitlab_access_request_list_project, gitlab_project_member_add

Returns: JSON with the approved access request details.

user_id.description: User ID of the access requester,required

name: gitlab_access_request_deny_group
description:
Deny a group access request. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_access_request_approve_group, gitlab_access_request_list_group

Returns: confirmation message.

user_id.description: User ID of the access requester,required

name: gitlab_access_request_deny_project
description:
Deny a project access request. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_access_request_approve_project, gitlab_access_request_list_project

Returns: confirmation message.

user_id.description: User ID of the access requester,required

name: gitlab_access_request_list_group
description:
List access requests for a GitLab group.

See also: gitlab_access_request_approve_group, gitlab_group_member_add

Returns: JSON array of access requests with pagination.

name: gitlab_access_request_list_project
description:
List access requests for a GitLab project.

See also: gitlab_access_request_approve_project, gitlab_project_member_add

Returns: JSON array of access requests with pagination.

name: gitlab_access_request_request_group
description:
Request access to a GitLab group for the authenticated user.

See also: gitlab_access_request_list_group, gitlab_group_member_add

Returns: JSON with the access request details.

name: gitlab_access_request_request_project
description:
Request access to a GitLab project for the authenticated user.

See also: gitlab_access_request_list_project, gitlab_project_member_add

Returns: JSON with the access request details.

name: gitlab_activate_user
description:
Activate a deactivated GitLab user (admin only). Deactivated users have been inactive and need reactivation.

See also: gitlab_deactivate_user, gitlab_get_user

Returns: JSON with action confirmation.

name: gitlab_add_commit_discussion_note
description:
Add a reply note to an existing commit discussion thread.

Returns: JSON with created note including ID, body, and author.

See also: gitlab_get_commit_discussion, gitlab_update_commit_discussion_note

name: gitlab_add_email
description:
Add an email address to the currently authenticated GitLab user.

See also: gitlab_list_emails, gitlab_delete_email

Returns: JSON with the created email details.

name: gitlab_add_email_for_user
description:
Add an email address to a specific GitLab user (admin only). Requires user ID and email address.

See also: gitlab_list_emails_for_user, gitlab_delete_email_for_user

Returns: JSON with the created email details.

name: gitlab_add_epic_discussion_note
description:
Add a reply note to an existing epic discussion thread via the Work Items GraphQL API.

Returns: JSON with created note including ID, body, and author.

See also: gitlab_get_epic_discussion, gitlab_update_epic_discussion_note

full_path.description: Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required

iid.description: Epic IID within the group,required

name: gitlab_add_gpg_key
description:
Add a GPG key to the currently authenticated GitLab user. Requires the armored GPG public key.

See also: gitlab_list_gpg_keys, gitlab_delete_gpg_key

Returns: JSON with the created GPG key details.

key.description: The armored GPG public key content,required

name: gitlab_add_gpg_key_for_user
description:
Add a GPG key to a specific GitLab user (admin only). Requires user ID and GPG public key.

See also: gitlab_list_gpg_keys_for_user, gitlab_delete_gpg_key_for_user

Returns: JSON with the created GPG key details.

key.description: The armored GPG public key content,required

name: gitlab_add_group_badge
description:
Add a new badge to a group. Badge URLs support placeholders like %{project_path}, %{default_branch}, %{commit_sha}.

See also: gitlab_list_group_badges, gitlab_preview_group_badge

Returns: JSON with the badge details.

name: gitlab_add_group_job_token_allowlist
description:
Add a group to the CI/CD job token allowlist.

Returns: JSON with the allowlist entry.

See also: gitlab_list_job_token_group_allowlist, gitlab_remove_group_job_token_allowlist

target_group_id.description: ID of the group to add to the allowlist,required

name: gitlab_add_issue_discussion_note
description:
Add a reply note to an existing issue discussion thread.

Returns: JSON with the created reply note. See also: gitlab_get_issue_discussion.

name: gitlab_add_license
description:
Add a new GitLab license (admin). Requires the Base64-encoded license string.

Returns: JSON with the added license details.

See also: gitlab_get_license.

name: gitlab_add_merge_request_to_merge_train
description:
Add a merge request to a merge train.

Returns: JSON array of merge train entries after the addition.

See also: gitlab_get_merge_request_on_merge_train, gitlab_list_project_merge_trains

sha.description: Head SHA of the merge request to verify

name: gitlab_add_project_badge
description:
Add a new badge to a project. Badge URLs support placeholders like %{project_path}, %{default_branch}, %{commit_sha}.

See also: gitlab_list_project_badges, gitlab_preview_project_badge

Returns: JSON with the badge details.

name: gitlab_add_project_job_token_allowlist
description:
Add a project to the CI/CD job token inbound allowlist.

Returns: JSON with the allowlist entry.

See also: gitlab_list_job_token_inbound_allowlist, gitlab_remove_project_job_token_allowlist

target_project_id.description: ID of the project to add to the allowlist,required

name: gitlab_add_project_mirror
description:
Create a new remote push mirror for a GitLab project. The mirror URL should include credentials if needed.

Returns: JSON with created mirror details. See also: gitlab_edit_project_mirror.

host_keys.description: SSH host keys for the remote mirror

keep_divergent_refs.description: Keep divergent refs on the remote

mirror_branch_regex.description: Regex pattern for branches to mirror

name: gitlab_add_snippet_discussion_note
description:
Add a reply note to an existing snippet discussion thread.

Returns: JSON with the created reply note.

See also: gitlab_get_snippet_discussion, gitlab_update_snippet_discussion_note

name: gitlab_add_ssh_key
description:
Add an SSH key to the currently authenticated GitLab user. Requires a title and the public key content.

See also: gitlab_list_ssh_keys, gitlab_delete_ssh_key

Returns: JSON with the created SSH key details.

title.description: A descriptive title for the SSH key,required

usage_type.description: Usage type: auth or signing (default: auth)

name: gitlab_add_ssh_key_for_user
description:
Add an SSH key to a specific GitLab user (admin only). Requires user ID, title, and public key content.

See also: gitlab_list_ssh_keys_for_user, gitlab_delete_ssh_key_for_user

Returns: JSON with the created SSH key details.

title.description: A descriptive title for the SSH key,required

usage_type.description: Usage type: auth or signing (default: auth)

name: gitlab_add_system_hook
description:
Add a new system hook (admin). Requires URL. Optionally configure event subscriptions and SSL verification.

Returns: JSON with the created system hook details.

See also: gitlab_list_system_hooks, gitlab_delete_system_hook

branch_filter_strategy.description: Branch filter strategy: wildcard, regex, or all_branches

enable_ssl_verification.description: Enable SSL verification for the hook URL

push_events_branch_filter.description: Branch filter for push events (wildcard, regex, or branch name)

name: gitlab_analyze_ci_configuration
description:
Analyze a GitLab project's CI/CD configuration using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Lints the CI config, fetches merged YAML and includes, then requests LLM analysis for best practices, performance, security, and maintainability. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown analysis of CI/CD configuration covering best practices, performance, security, and maintainability.

See also: gitlab_ci_lint_project, gitlab_list_pipelines

content_ref.description: Branch or tag for CI content (defaults to default branch)

name: gitlab_analyze_deployment_history
description:
Analyze deployment history and patterns for a GitLab project using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Fetches recent deployments, then requests LLM to assess deployment frequency, success rate, rollback patterns, and suggest improvements. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown analysis of deployment patterns with frequency, success rate, and improvement suggestions.

See also: gitlab_list_deployments, gitlab_list_environments

environment.description: Filter by environment name (e.g. production, staging)

name: gitlab_analyze_issue_scope
description:
Analyze a GitLab issue's scope and effort using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Fetches issue details, time stats, participants, related MRs, and discussion notes, then requests LLM to assess scope, complexity, risks, and whether the issue should be broken down. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown analysis of issue scope, complexity, risks, and breakdown recommendations.

See also: gitlab_summarize_issue, gitlab_get_issue

name: gitlab_analyze_mr_changes
description:
Analyze a GitLab merge request using LLM-assisted code review via MCP sampling. Fetches MR details and diffs, then requests LLM analysis for code quality, bugs, and improvements. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown analysis of merge request changes including code quality, bugs, and improvement recommendations.

See also: gitlab_review_mr_security, gitlab_summarize_mr_review

name: gitlab_analyze_pipeline_failure
description:
Analyze a GitLab pipeline failure using LLM-assisted root cause analysis via MCP sampling. Fetches pipeline details, failed jobs and their traces, then requests LLM analysis for root cause, fix suggestions, and impact assessment. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown analysis of pipeline failure with root cause and suggested fixes.

See also: gitlab_get_pipeline, gitlab_get_job_trace

name: gitlab_approve_user
description:
Approve a pending GitLab user registration (admin only). Required when user sign-up requires admin approval.

See also: gitlab_reject_user, gitlab_list_users

Returns: JSON with action confirmation.

name: gitlab_ban_user
description:
Ban a GitLab user, hiding their activity and preventing login (admin only). More restrictive than blocking.

See also: gitlab_unban_user, gitlab_block_user

Returns: JSON with action confirmation.

name: gitlab_block_user
description:
Block a GitLab user, preventing login and API access (admin only). The user's contributions remain intact.

See also: gitlab_unblock_user, gitlab_get_user

Returns: JSON with action confirmation.

name: gitlab_board_create
description:
Create a new issue board in a project

See also: gitlab_board_list, gitlab_board_list_create

Returns: JSON with the board details.

name: gitlab_board_delete
description:
Delete an issue board from a project. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_board_list, gitlab_board_create

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_board_get
description:
Get a single issue board

See also: gitlab_board_list, gitlab_board_list_lists

Returns: JSON with board details.

name: gitlab_board_list
description:
List all issue boards for a project

See also: gitlab_board_create, gitlab_list_issues

Returns: JSON array of boards with pagination.

name: gitlab_board_list_create
description:
Create a new list in an issue board

See also: gitlab_board_list_lists, gitlab_list_labels

Returns: JSON with the board list details.

assignee_id.description: Assignee ID to create an assignee list

iteration_id.description: Iteration ID to create an iteration list

label_id.description: Label ID to create a label list

milestone_id.description: Milestone ID to create a milestone list

name: gitlab_board_list_delete
description:
Delete a list from an issue board. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_board_list_lists, gitlab_board_list_create

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_board_list_get
description:
Get a single list from an issue board

See also: gitlab_board_list_lists, gitlab_board_list_update

Returns: JSON with board list details.

name: gitlab_board_list_lists
description:
List all lists in an issue board

See also: gitlab_board_list_create, gitlab_board_get

Returns: JSON array of board lists with pagination.

name: gitlab_board_list_update
description:
Update (reorder) a list in an issue board

See also: gitlab_board_list_get, gitlab_board_list_lists

Returns: JSON with the board list details.

name: gitlab_board_update
description:
Update an existing issue board

See also: gitlab_board_get, gitlab_board_list

Returns: JSON with the board details.

name: gitlab_branch_create
description: Create a new Git branch in a GitLab project from a ref (branch name, tag name, or commit SHA). Returns: branch name, merged/protected/default status, web URL, and latest commit ID. See also: gitlab_mr_create, gitlab_branch_list.

ref.description: Branch name, tag, or commit SHA to create from,required

name: gitlab_branch_delete
description:
Delete a branch from a GitLab repository. Cannot delete the default branch or protected branches.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_branch_list, gitlab_branch_create.

name: gitlab_branch_delete_merged
description:
Delete all branches that have been merged into the default branch. The default branch and protected branches are never deleted.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_branch_list, gitlab_mr_list.

name: gitlab_branch_get
description:
Retrieve detailed information about a single branch in a GitLab project. Returns branch name, merged/protected/default status, web URL, and latest commit ID.

Returns: name, merged, protected, default status, web_url, and latest commit_id. See also: gitlab_branch_create, gitlab_commit_list.

name: gitlab_branch_list
description:
List Git branches in a GitLab project. Supports optional name search filter. Returns paginated results including each branch's protection status and latest commit info.

Returns: paginated list of branches with name, protected status, and latest commit info. See also: gitlab_branch_get, gitlab_branch_create.

name: gitlab_branch_protect
description: Protect a GitLab repository branch by setting push and merge access levels (0=no access, 30=developer, 40=maintainer, 60=admin). Protected branches cannot be force-pushed or deleted. Returns: branch name, push/merge access levels, allow_force_push, and code_owner_approval_required. See also: gitlab_branch_unprotect, gitlab_protected_branches_list.

allow_force_push.description: Allow force push to this branch

code_owner_approval_required.description: Require CODEOWNERS approval for changes to matching files

name: gitlab_branch_unprotect
description:
Remove all protection rules from a GitLab branch, allowing unrestricted push, merge, and force-push access.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_branch_protect, gitlab_protected_branches_list.

branch_name.description: Name of the protected branch to remove,required

name: gitlab_cancel_github_import
description:
Cancel an ongoing GitHub project import

Returns: JSON with cancellation result.

See also: gitlab_import_from_github, gitlab_get_project_import_status

project_id.description: The GitLab project ID of the import to cancel,required

name: gitlab_change_plan_limits
description:
Change plan limits (admin). Requires plan_name; optionally set individual file size limits.

Returns: JSON with the updated plan limits.

See also: gitlab_get_plan_limits.

conan_max_file_size.description: Maximum Conan package file size in bytes

generic_packages_max_file_size.description: Maximum generic package file size in bytes

helm_max_file_size.description: Maximum Helm chart file size in bytes

maven_max_file_size.description: Maximum Maven package file size in bytes

npm_max_file_size.description: Maximum NPM package file size in bytes

nuget_max_file_size.description: Maximum NuGet package file size in bytes

plan_name.description: Plan name to update (e.g. default, free, bronze, silver, gold, premium, ultimate),required

pypi_max_file_size.description: Maximum PyPI package file size in bytes

terraform_module_max_file_size.description: Maximum Terraform module file size in bytes

name: gitlab_ci_lint
description:
Validate arbitrary CI/CD YAML content within a project's namespace context. Useful for testing CI configuration changes before committing. Returns validation status, errors, warnings, and merged YAML.

Returns: JSON with validation status, errors, warnings, and merged YAML.

See also: gitlab_ci_lint_project, gitlab_list_ci_yml_templates

include_jobs.description: Include expanded job list in the response

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path (namespace context),required

ref.description: Branch or tag to use for CI includes resolution

name: gitlab_ci_lint_project
description:
Validate a project's CI/CD configuration (.gitlab-ci.yml) from the repository. Returns validation status, errors, warnings, merged YAML, and includes.

Returns: JSON with validation status, errors, warnings, and merged YAML.

See also: gitlab_ci_lint, gitlab_list_ci_yml_templates

content_ref.description: Branch or tag to use for the CI configuration content

dry_run_ref.description: Branch or tag to use as context for the dry run

include_jobs.description: Include expanded job list in the response

ref.description: Branch or tag to use for CI includes resolution

name: gitlab_ci_variable_create
description: Create a new CI/CD variable in a GitLab project. Requires key and value. Optionally set type (env_var/file), protection, masking, and environment scope. Returns: key, value, variable_type, protected, masked, environment_scope. See also: gitlab_ci_variable_list.

masked.description: Mask variable value in job logs

raw.description: Treat variable value as raw string

name: gitlab_ci_variable_delete
description:
Delete a CI/CD variable from a GitLab project by key. Optionally filter by environment scope. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_ci_variable_list.

name: gitlab_ci_variable_get
description: Get a specific CI/CD variable by key from a GitLab project. Optionally filter by environment scope when duplicate keys exist. Returns: key, value, variable_type, protected, masked, environment_scope. See also: gitlab_ci_variable_list.

name: gitlab_ci_variable_list
description:
List CI/CD variables for a GitLab project. Returns paginated results with variable key, type, protection, masking, and environment scope.

Returns: paginated list of variables with key, variable_type, protected, masked, and environment_scope. See also: gitlab_ci_variable_get, gitlab_pipeline_list.

name: gitlab_ci_variable_update
description: Update an existing CI/CD variable in a GitLab project. Specify the key to update and any fields to change: value, type, protection, masking, environment scope. Returns: key, value, variable_type, protected, masked, environment_scope. See also: gitlab_ci_variable_get.

masked.description: Mask variable value in job logs

raw.description: Treat variable value as raw string

name: gitlab_commit_cherry_pick
description: Cherry-pick a commit to a target branch. Supports dry_run to check for conflicts without creating the commit, and custom commit message. Returns: id, short_id, title, author_name, committed_date, web_url. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_branch_create.

dry_run.description: If true, does not create the commit but checks for conflicts

name: gitlab_commit_comment_create
description:
Post a comment on a commit. Supports file-level inline comments with path and line number.

Returns: note, author, path, and line of the created comment. See also: gitlab_commit_comments.

line_type.description: Line type: new or old (default: new)

path.description: File path to comment on (for inline comments)

name: gitlab_commit_comments
description:
List comments on a specific commit. Returns comment text, file path, line number, and author with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of comments with note, path, line, and author. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

name: gitlab_commit_create
description: Create a Git commit with one or more file actions in a GitLab repository. Each action in the 'actions' array requires: 'action' (create/update/delete/move/chmod), 'file_path', and 'content' (required for create and update). For 'move' actions, also set 'previous_path'. Supports multi-file atomic commits on any branch with optional author override and start_branch to create new branches. Returns: id, short_id, title, author_name, committed_date, web_url. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_branch_create.

actions.description: List of file actions (create, update, delete, move),required

action.description: Action to perform on the file (create, update, delete, move),required

content.description: File content (required for create and update)

file_path.description: Full path of the file (e.g. src/main.go),required

last_commit_id.description: Last known commit ID of this file for conflict detection

previous_path.description: Original path when action is move

force.description: When true, force-overwrite the target branch even if a conflict exists

start_branch.description: Branch to start from if target branch does not exist

start_sha.description: SHA to start from if target branch does not exist (alternative to start_branch)

name: gitlab_commit_diff
description:
List the diffs (changed files) for a specific commit in a GitLab project. Returns old/new paths, diff text, and flags for new/renamed/deleted files with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of diffs with old_path, new_path, diff text, and new/renamed/deleted flags. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

sha.description: Commit SHA hash to get diffs for,required

unidiff.description: Return diffs in unified diff format

name: gitlab_commit_get
description:
Retrieve a single commit by SHA from a GitLab project. Returns commit ID, title, full message, author/committer info, parent IDs, stats (additions/deletions/total), and web URL. See also: gitlab_commit_diff, gitlab_commit_merge_requests.

Returns: id, short_id, title, message, author, committer, parent_ids, stats, and web_url.

name: gitlab_commit_list
description:
List commits in a GitLab repository. Supports filtering by branch/tag (ref_name), date range (since/until in ISO 8601), file path, and author. Optionally includes commit stats (additions/deletions). Returns commit ID, title, author, date, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of commits with id, short_id, title, author_name, authored_date, and web_url. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_branch_list.

author.description: Filter by commit author name or email

first_parent.description: Follow only the first parent commit upon seeing a merge commit

path.description: File path to filter commits by (only commits touching this path)

ref_name.description: Branch name, tag, or commit SHA to list commits from (default: default branch)

since.description: Return commits after this date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)

until.description: Return commits before this date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)

name: gitlab_commit_merge_requests
description:
List merge requests associated with a commit. Returns MR IID, title, state, source/target branches, author, and web URL.

Returns: list of merge requests with iid, title, state, source/target branches, author, and web_url. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_mr_list.

name: gitlab_commit_refs
description:
Get branches and tags a commit is pushed to. Returns ref type (branch/tag) and name. Supports filtering by type and pagination.

Returns: paginated list of refs with type (branch/tag) and name. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_branch_list.

type.description: Filter by ref type: branch, tag, or all (default: all)

name: gitlab_commit_revert
description: Revert a commit on a target branch. Creates a new commit that undoes the changes of the specified commit. Returns: id, short_id, title, author_name, committed_date, web_url. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_branch_create.

name: gitlab_commit_signature
description:
Get the GPG signature of a commit if it was signed. Returns verification status, key ID, user name, and email.

Returns: verification_status, gpg_key_id, gpg_key_user_name, and gpg_key_user_email. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

name: gitlab_commit_status_set
description: Set the pipeline status of a commit. State can be: pending, running, success, failed, or canceled. Supports optional ref, name, target_url, description, coverage, and pipeline_id. Returns: id, sha, ref, status, name, description, coverage, pipeline_id. See also: gitlab_commit_statuses.

pipeline_id.description: Pipeline ID to associate the status with

ref.description: Branch or tag name

state.description: Status state: pending, running, success, failed, canceled,required

target_url.description: URL to link from the status

name: gitlab_commit_statuses
description:
List pipeline statuses of a commit. Returns status state, name, ref, description, and coverage. Supports filtering by ref, stage, name, and pipeline_id with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of statuses with id, state, name, ref, description, and coverage. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

ref.description: Branch or tag name filter

name: gitlab_confirm_vulnerability
description: Confirm a detected vulnerability (requires Ultimate/Premium). Changes state from DETECTED to CONFIRMED. Returns: updated vulnerability state. See also: gitlab_get_vulnerability, gitlab_dismiss_vulnerability, gitlab_resolve_vulnerability.

name: gitlab_create_application
description:
Create an OAuth2 application (admin). Params: name (required), redirect_uri (required), scopes (required), confidential.

Returns: JSON with the created application details.

See also: gitlab_list_applications, gitlab_delete_application

name: gitlab_create_broadcast_message
description:
Create a broadcast message. Requires admin access.

Returns: JSON with the created broadcast message details.

See also: gitlab_list_broadcast_messages, gitlab_update_broadcast_message

target_path.description: Target path to show message on

theme.description: Theme: indigo, light-indigo, blue, light-blue, green, light-green, red, light-red

name: gitlab_create_cluster_agent_token
description:
Create a token for a cluster agent

See also: gitlab_list_cluster_agent_tokens, gitlab_get_cluster_agent

Returns: JSON with the created token details.

name: gitlab_create_commit_discussion
description:
Create a new discussion thread on a project commit. Supports inline diff comments via position.

Returns: JSON with created discussion thread including ID and initial note.

See also: gitlab_list_commit_discussions, gitlab_get_commit

name: gitlab_create_current_user_pat
description:
Create a personal access token for the currently authenticated GitLab user. Requires token name and scopes.

See also: gitlab_user_current

Returns: JSON with the created token (includes the token value).

name.description: Name of the personal access token,required

name: gitlab_create_custom_emoji
description: Create a custom emoji in a GitLab group via GraphQL API. Requires group path, emoji name (without colons), and image URL. Returns: created emoji with ID, name, URL, and created date. See also: gitlab_list_custom_emoji, gitlab_delete_custom_emoji.

url.description: required,URL to the emoji image (PNG or GIF recommended)

name: gitlab_create_dependency_list_export
description: Create a dependency list export (SBOM) for a pipeline. Returns: export ID and status. Use gitlab_get_dependency_list_export to check status, then gitlab_download_dependency_list_export to download. See also: gitlab_get_dependency_list_export, gitlab_download_dependency_list_export.

pipeline_id.description: Pipeline ID to export dependencies from,required

name: gitlab_create_epic_discussion
description:
Create a new discussion thread on a group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API.

Returns: JSON with created discussion thread including ID and initial note.

See also: gitlab_list_epic_discussions, gitlab_add_epic_discussion_note

full_path.description: Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required

name: gitlab_discover_project
description:
Resolve a git remote URL to a GitLab project and return its project_id and metadata. Read-only; performs at most one authenticated GET /projects/:path lookup; no side effects.

When to use: at the start of a workspace session, to obtain the project_id required by most other gitlab_* tools. Extract the FULL remote URL from .git/config ([remote "origin"] url = ...) or from 'git remote -v'.
NOT for: searching projects by name (use gitlab_search action=projects), listing a user's projects (use gitlab_user action=list_projects), verifying GitLab connectivity or authentication (use gitlab_server action=health_check).

IMPORTANT: pass the complete URL exactly as it appears — do NOT strip the git@ prefix from SSH URLs. Supported formats:
- HTTPS: https://gitlab.example.com/group/project.git
- SSH shorthand: git@gitlab.example.com:group/project.git
- SSH protocol: ssh://git@gitlab.example.com/group/project.git
- Bare path: gitlab.example.com/group/project

Returns: {id, name, path_with_namespace, web_url, description, default_branch, visibility, archived}. Errors: 404 not found (hint: project may be private — verify token permissions), 403 forbidden (hint: token lacks read_api scope).

See also: gitlab_project (full project CRUD/settings once id is known), gitlab_server (connectivity and version checks), gitlab_search (find projects by query).

remote_url.description: Full git remote URL (HTTPS or SSH) exactly as shown in .git/config or 'git remote -v' output. IMPORTANT: pass the complete URL including the scheme (https://) or user prefix (git@). Examples: 'https://gitlab.example.com/group/project.git' or 'git@gitlab.example.com:group/project.git'. Do NOT strip the git@ prefix from SSH URLs.,required

name: gitlab_file_get
description:
Retrieve the decoded content of a single file from a GitLab repository at a specific ref (branch name, tag name, or commit SHA). Returns file content, size, encoding, and last commit ID. This is the primary tool for reading file contents. For raw text without metadata use gitlab_file_raw. For metadata only (no content) use gitlab_file_metadata.

Returns: file_name, file_path, size, encoding, content, ref, blob_id, commit_id, and last_commit_id. See also: gitlab_file_create, gitlab_repository_tree.

file_path.description: URL-encoded full path of the file (e.g. src%2Fmain.go or src/main.go),required

Get file metadata WITHOUT retrieving content. Use this to check file existence or properties; for content use gitlab_file_get. Returns: file_name, size, encoding, blob_id, commit_id, last_commit_id, and SHA-256.

See also: gitlab_file_get.

name: gitlab_file_raw_metadata

Get file metadata via HEAD request to the raw file endpoint. Returns size, encoding, blob ID, commit IDs, and SHA-256 without retrieving content. Lighter than gitlab_file_metadata — uses a HEAD request instead of GET.

Returns: file_name, size, encoding, blob_id, commit_id, last_commit_id, and SHA-256.

file_path.description: URL-encoded full path of the file,required

name: gitlab_find_technical_debt
description:
Find and analyze technical debt in a GitLab project using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Searches for TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX, and DEPRECATED markers in source code, then requests LLM to categorize, prioritize, and recommend a remediation strategy. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown report of technical debt categorized by priority with remediation strategy.

See also: gitlab_search_code, gitlab_get_project

ref.description: Branch or tag to search in (defaults to default branch)

Generate a comprehensive milestone progress report using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Fetches milestone details, linked issues and merge requests, then requests LLM to produce a data-driven progress report with metrics, risks, and recommendations. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown progress report with metrics, risks, and recommendations.

See also: gitlab_get_milestone, gitlab_list_milestone_issues

name: gitlab_generate_release_notes

Generate polished release notes using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Compares two Git refs, fetches commits and merged MRs with labels, then requests LLM to produce categorized release notes (Features, Bug Fixes, Improvements, Breaking Changes). Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown release notes categorized by Features, Bug Fixes, Improvements, and Breaking Changes.

See also: gitlab_create_release, gitlab_list_commits
