IMPORTANT: if the user does not specify a target branch, retrieve the project default branch via gitlab_project_get and use its default_branch value — do NOT assume 'main'. Supports title, Markdown description, assignee IDs, reviewer IDs, labels, milestone ID, allow_collaboration, and target_project_id (for cross-project/fork MRs). The squash and remove_source_branch options are omitted by default to preserve repository-level settings; only set them when the user explicitly requests it.

Returns: JSON with the created merge request details including iid, title, state, source/target branches, author, and web_url. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_project_get, gitlab_branch_create.

allow_collaboration.description: Allow commits from upstream members who can merge to target branch

assignee_id.description: Single user ID to assign (use assignee_ids for multiple)

milestone_id.description: Milestone ID to associate with the merge request

remove_source_branch.description: Delete source branch after merge. Only set if explicitly requested by the user. Omit to preserve repository defaults

reviewer_ids.description: User IDs to add as reviewers

squash.description: Squash commits on merge. Only set if explicitly requested by the user. Omit to preserve repository defaults

target_branch.description: Target branch name. If not specified by the user use the project default branch from gitlab_project_get (do NOT assume main),required

name: gitlab_mr_create_pipeline
description:
Create a new pipeline for a GitLab merge request. Triggers a CI/CD pipeline run on the MR's source branch. Returns the created pipeline details.

Returns: JSON with the created pipeline details including id, status, ref, sha, and web_url. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_mr_get.

name: gitlab_mr_create_todo
description:
Create a to-do item on a GitLab merge request for the authenticated user. Adds the MR to the user's to-do list for later follow-up.

Returns: JSON with the created to-do item details including id, target type, and action name. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

name: gitlab_mr_delete
description:
Permanently delete a GitLab merge request. This action cannot be undone. Requires at least Maintainer access level.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_list.

mr_iid.description: Merge request IID to delete (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required

name: gitlab_mr_dependencies_list
description:
List all merge request dependencies (blockers) for a GitLab merge request. Returns the list of MRs that must be merged before this MR can be merged. Requires Premium or Ultimate license.

Returns: JSON array of blocking merge requests with iid, title, state, and web_url. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

name: gitlab_mr_dependency_create
description:
Create a merge request dependency (blocker). The specified blocking MR must be merged before this MR can be merged. Requires Premium or Ultimate license.

Returns: JSON with the dependency details including blocking merge request information. See also: gitlab_mr_dependencies_list.

blocking_merge_request_id.description: ID of the merge request that blocks this one

name: gitlab_mr_dependency_delete
description:
Remove a merge request dependency (blocker). The specified blocking MR will no longer prevent this MR from being merged. Requires Premium or Ultimate license.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_dependencies_list.

blocking_merge_request_id.description: ID of the blocking merge request to remove

name: gitlab_mr_diff_version_get
description:
Get a single merge request diff version with its commits and file diffs. Use the version_id from gitlab_mr_diff_versions_list. Optionally request unified diff format.

Returns: JSON with diff version commits and file diffs. See also: gitlab_mr_diff_versions_list.

unidiff.description: Return diffs in unified diff format (default: false)

name: gitlab_mr_diff_versions_list
description:
List all diff versions (historical snapshots) of a merge request. Returns version IDs, SHAs, state, and timestamps — NOT the actual diffs. Use gitlab_mr_diff_version_get with a version_id to retrieve diffs for a specific version. For current diffs, use gitlab_mr_changes_get instead.

Returns: JSON array of diff versions with pagination. See also: gitlab_mr_changes_get.

name: gitlab_mr_discussion_create
description:
Start a new threaded discussion on a GitLab merge request. For a general discussion, just provide 'body'. For an inline diff comment, also provide 'position' with base_sha, start_sha, head_sha (get these from gitlab_mr_get diff_refs field), new_path, and old_line/new_line.

Returns: JSON with the created discussion. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_discussion_list.

position.description: Diff position for inline comments. Omit for general MR discussions.

head_sha.description: HEAD commit SHA of the MR source branch,required

new_line.description: Line in new file. Set ONLY for added or modified lines. For removed lines use old_line instead. Set both old_line and new_line only for unchanged context lines.

old_line.description: Line in old file. Set ONLY for removed lines. For modified or added lines use new_line instead. Set both old_line and new_line only for unchanged context lines.

old_path.description: File path before the change (for modified/deleted files)

start_sha.description: SHA of the first commit in the MR,required

name: gitlab_mr_discussion_get
description:
Get a single discussion thread from a GitLab merge request by its discussion ID, including all notes in the thread.

Returns: JSON with discussion details and notes. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_list.

name: gitlab_mr_discussion_list
description:
List all discussion threads on a GitLab merge request including inline diff comments and general discussions. Each thread contains its notes and resolution status. Returns paginated results.

Returns: JSON array of discussions with pagination. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_note_list.

name: gitlab_mr_discussion_note_delete
description:
Delete a note from a merge request discussion thread. Only the note author or project maintainers can delete notes.

Returns: JSON confirming note deletion. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_get.

discussion_id.description: ID of the discussion containing the note,required

note_id.description: ID of the note to delete,required

name: gitlab_mr_discussion_note_update
description:
Update the body or resolved status of a note within a merge request discussion thread. You can modify the text, change resolution status, or both.

Returns: JSON with the updated note details. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_get.

body.description: New body text (Markdown). Leave empty to keep current body.

discussion_id.description: ID of the discussion containing the note,required

note_id.description: ID of the note to update,required

resolved.description: Set to true to resolve, false to unresolve. Omit to leave unchanged.

name: gitlab_mr_discussion_reply
description:
Add a reply to an existing discussion thread on a GitLab merge request. The reply appears nested under the original discussion note.

Returns: JSON with the created reply note. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_get.

discussion_id.description: ID of the discussion to reply to,required

name: gitlab_mr_discussion_resolve
description:
Resolve or unresolve a discussion thread on a GitLab merge request. Resolved discussions are collapsed in the UI to reduce review noise.

Returns: JSON with the updated discussion resolution status. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_get.

discussion_id.description: ID of the discussion to resolve,required

resolved.description: True to resolve, false to unresolve,required

name: gitlab_mr_draft_note_create
description:
Create a new draft note (pending review comment) on a GitLab merge request. Supports inline diff comments on specific lines via the position parameter. Draft notes stay private until published with draft_note_publish_all. Prefer this over discussion_create for code reviews to batch all comments into a single notification.

Returns: JSON with the created draft note details. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

commit_id.description: SHA of the commit to comment on

position.description: Diff position for inline comments on specific lines. Omit for general MR comments.

head_sha.description: HEAD commit SHA of the MR source branch,required

new_line.description: Line in new file. Set ONLY for added or modified lines. For removed lines use old_line instead. Set both old_line and new_line only for unchanged context lines.

old_line.description: Line in old file. Set ONLY for removed lines. For modified or added lines use new_line instead. Set both old_line and new_line only for unchanged context lines.

old_path.description: File path before the change (for modified/deleted files)

start_sha.description: SHA of the first commit in the MR,required

name: gitlab_mr_draft_note_delete
description:
Permanently delete a draft note from a GitLab merge request. Only the draft author can delete it.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_list.

name: gitlab_mr_draft_note_get
description:
Get a single draft note from a GitLab merge request by its ID. Returns the note body, author, and associated commit/discussion details.

Returns: JSON with draft note details. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_list.

name: gitlab_mr_draft_note_list
description:
List all draft notes (pending review comments) on a GitLab merge request. Draft notes are only visible to the author until published. Supports pagination and sorting.

Returns: JSON array of draft notes. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_discussion_list.

order_by.description: Order by: id (default)

name: gitlab_mr_draft_note_publish
description:
Publish a single draft note on a GitLab merge request, making it visible to all participants. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_get.

name: gitlab_mr_draft_note_publish_all
description:
Publish all pending draft notes on a GitLab merge request at once, making them visible to all participants. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_list.

name: gitlab_mr_draft_note_update
description:
Update the body text of an existing draft note on a GitLab merge request. Only the draft author can update it.

Returns: JSON with the updated draft note details. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_get.

position.description: Updated diff position for inline comments

head_sha.description: HEAD commit SHA of the MR source branch,required

new_line.description: Line in new file. Set ONLY for added or modified lines. For removed lines use old_line instead. Set both old_line and new_line only for unchanged context lines.

old_line.description: Line in old file. Set ONLY for removed lines. For modified or added lines use new_line instead. Set both old_line and new_line only for unchanged context lines.

old_path.description: File path before the change (for modified/deleted files)

start_sha.description: SHA of the first commit in the MR,required

name: gitlab_mr_emoji_create
description:
Add an award emoji reaction to a merge request.

See also: gitlab_mr_emoji_list, gitlab_mr_emoji_delete

Returns: JSON with the created award emoji.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_mr_emoji_delete
description:
Delete an award emoji from a merge request.

See also: gitlab_mr_emoji_list, gitlab_mr_emoji_create

Returns: confirmation message.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_mr_emoji_get
description:
Get a single award emoji on a merge request.

See also: gitlab_mr_emoji_list, gitlab_mr_emoji_create

Returns: JSON with award emoji details including id, name, user, and created_at.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_mr_emoji_list
description:
List all award emoji on a merge request.

See also: gitlab_mr_emoji_create, gitlab_issue_emoji_list

Returns: JSON array of award emoji with pagination. Fields include id, name, user, and awardable_type.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)

name: gitlab_mr_get
description:
Retrieve detailed information about a GitLab merge request by its IID (project-scoped ID), including title, description, state, source/target branches, author, assignees, reviewers, labels, and pipeline status. See also: gitlab_mr_changes_get, gitlab_mr_discussion_list.

Returns: JSON with merge request details including iid, title, description, state, author, assignees, reviewers, labels, pipeline status, and merge status.

name: gitlab_mr_issues_closed
description:
List all issues that would be closed when a GitLab merge request is merged. Returns issue details including IID, title, state, author, and labels with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of issues with pagination. Fields include iid, title, state, author, and labels. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_issue_get.

name: gitlab_mr_label_event_get
description:
Get a single label event for a merge request.

Returns: JSON with label event details including id, action, user, created_at.

See also: gitlab_mr_label_event_list, gitlab_mr_milestone_event_get

name: gitlab_mr_label_event_list
description:
List label events for a merge request. Shows when labels were added or removed.

Returns: JSON array of label events with pagination. Fields include id, action, user, created_at.

See also: gitlab_mr_label_event_get, gitlab_list_merge_requests

name: gitlab_mr_list
description:
List merge requests in a GitLab project. Supports filtering by state (opened/closed/merged/all), author, assignee, reviewer, labels, milestone, and source/target branch. Returns paginated results.

Returns: JSON array of merge requests with pagination. Fields include iid, title, state, author, source/target branches, and web_url. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_create.

created_after.description: Return MRs created after date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)

created_before.description: Return MRs created before date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)

draft.description: Filter by draft status (true=only drafts, false=only non-drafts)

iids.description: Filter by merge request internal IDs

labels.description: Comma-separated label names to filter by

order_by.description: Order by field (created_at, updated_at, title)

state.description: Filter by state (opened, closed, merged, all)

updated_after.description: Return MRs updated after date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)

updated_before.description: Return MRs updated before date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)

name: gitlab_mr_list_global
description:
List merge requests across all projects visible to the authenticated user. Supports filtering by state (opened/closed/merged/all), author, reviewer, labels, milestone, draft status, and date ranges. Returns paginated results.

Returns: JSON array of merge requests with pagination. Fields include iid, title, state, author, source/target branches, and web_url. See also: gitlab_mr_list, gitlab_mr_get.

draft.description: Filter by draft status (true=only drafts, false=only non-drafts)

labels.description: Comma-separated label names to filter by

order_by.description: Order by field (created_at, updated_at)

state.description: Filter by state (opened, closed, merged, all)

name: gitlab_mr_list_group
description:
List merge requests in a GitLab group. Supports filtering by state (opened/closed/merged/all), author, reviewer, labels, milestone, draft status, and date ranges. Returns paginated results.

Returns: JSON array of merge requests with pagination. Fields include iid, title, state, author, source/target branches, and web_url. See also: gitlab_mr_list, gitlab_mr_get.

draft.description: Filter by draft status (true=only drafts, false=only non-drafts)

labels.description: Comma-separated label names to filter by

order_by.description: Order by field (created_at, updated_at)

state.description: Filter by state (opened, closed, merged, all)

name: gitlab_mr_merge
description:
Merge an accepted GitLab merge request into its target branch. Supports optional squash commits, custom merge commit message, and automatic source branch deletion after merge. The server automatically detects enforced project settings (squash_on_merge, force_remove_source_branch) and applies them — you do NOT need to set squash or should_remove_source_branch unless the user explicitly requests a specific value.

Returns: JSON with the merged merge request details including final state and merge commit SHA. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_pipeline_list.

sha.description: Head SHA of the merge request — merge only if HEAD matches (safety check)

should_remove_source_branch.description: Delete source branch after merge. Only set if explicitly requested by the user. Omit to preserve repository defaults

squash.description: Squash commits on merge. Only set if explicitly requested by the user. Omit to preserve repository defaults

squash_commit_message.description: Custom squash commit message (used when squash is enabled)

name: gitlab_mr_milestone_event_get
description:
Get a single milestone event for a merge request.

Returns: JSON with milestone event details including id, action, user, created_at.

See also: gitlab_mr_milestone_event_list, gitlab_mr_state_event_get

name: gitlab_mr_milestone_event_list
description:
List milestone events for a merge request. Shows when milestones were added or removed.

Returns: JSON array of milestone events with pagination. Fields include id, action, user, created_at.

See also: gitlab_mr_milestone_event_get, gitlab_mr_label_event_list

name: gitlab_mr_note_create
description:
Add a comment (note) to a GitLab merge request. The comment appears in the merge request's activity timeline as a top-level note.

Returns: JSON with the created note (ID, author, body, timestamps). See also: gitlab_mr_get.

name: gitlab_mr_note_delete
description:
Permanently delete a comment from a GitLab merge request. Only the note author or a project maintainer can delete a note.

Returns: JSON confirmation of deletion. See also: gitlab_mr_note_get.

note_id.description: ID of the note to delete,required

name: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_create
description:
Add an award emoji reaction to a merge request note.

See also: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_list, gitlab_mr_note_emoji_delete

Returns: JSON with the created award emoji.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_delete
description:
Delete an award emoji from a merge request note.

See also: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_list, gitlab_mr_note_emoji_create

Returns: confirmation message.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_get
description:
Get a single award emoji on a merge request note.

See also: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_list, gitlab_mr_note_emoji_create

Returns: JSON with award emoji details including id, name, user, and created_at.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_list
description:
List all award emoji on a merge request note.

See also: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_create, gitlab_mr_emoji_list

Returns: JSON array of award emoji with pagination. Fields include id, name, user, and awardable_type.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)

name: gitlab_mr_note_get
description:
Get a single comment (note) from a GitLab merge request by its note ID, including author, timestamps, resolution status, and body.

Returns: JSON with note details (ID, author, body, timestamps, resolution status). See also: gitlab_mr_notes_list.

note_id.description: ID of the note to retrieve,required

name: gitlab_mr_note_update
description:
Edit the body text of an existing comment on a GitLab merge request. Only the note author or a project maintainer can update a note.

Returns: JSON with the updated note details. See also: gitlab_mr_note_get.

note_id.description: ID of the note to update,required

name: gitlab_mr_notes_list
description:
List all comments (notes) on a GitLab merge request ordered by creation date. Includes both user comments and system-generated notes (status changes, label updates).

Returns: JSON with array of notes and pagination info. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_discussion_list.

order_by.description: Order by field (created_at, updated_at)

name: gitlab_mr_participants
description:
List all participants (users who have interacted via comments, approvals, or commits) in a GitLab merge request. This includes anyone who participated, not just assigned reviewers. For assigned reviewers with review state, use gitlab_mr_reviewers instead.

Returns: JSON array of participant users with id, username, name, and avatar_url. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

name: gitlab_mr_pipelines
description:
List all pipelines associated with a GitLab merge request. Returns pipeline ID, status, source, ref, SHA, and web URL.

Returns: JSON array of pipelines with pagination. Fields include id, status, source, ref, sha, and web_url. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_pipeline_get.

name: gitlab_mr_raw_diffs
description:
Get the raw unified-diff output for a merge request in plain-text git-apply format. Use this for programmatic diff analysis or patch application. For human-readable structured diffs, use gitlab_mr_changes_get instead.

Returns: plain-text unified diff output.

See also: gitlab_mr_changes_get.

name: gitlab_mr_rebase
description:
Rebase a merge request's source branch against its target branch. Optionally skip triggering CI pipeline after rebase. Returns whether the rebase is in progress.

Returns: JSON with rebase_in_progress status. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

mr_iid.description: Merge request IID to rebase (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required

skip_ci.description: Skip triggering CI pipeline after rebase

name: gitlab_mr_related_issues
description:
List all issues related to a GitLab merge request (mentioned or linked). Returns issue details including IID, title, state, author, and labels with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of related issues with pagination. Fields include iid, title, state, author, and labels. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_issue_get.

name: gitlab_mr_reset_spent_time
description:
Reset the total spent time for a GitLab merge request to zero. Returns updated time stats.

Returns: JSON with time stats including time_estimate, total_time_spent, and human-readable formats. See also: gitlab_mr_time_stats.

name: gitlab_mr_reset_time_estimate
description:
Reset the time estimate for a GitLab merge request to zero. Returns updated time stats.

Returns: JSON with time stats including time_estimate, total_time_spent, and human-readable formats. See also: gitlab_mr_time_stats.

name: gitlab_mr_reviewers
description:
List the explicitly assigned reviewers of a GitLab merge request with their review state (approved/reviewed/unreviewed) and assignment date. For all users who interacted (comments, commits, approvals), use gitlab_mr_participants instead.

Returns: JSON array of reviewers with id, username, name, state, and created_at. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_approve.

name: gitlab_mr_set_time_estimate
description:
Set the time estimate for a GitLab merge request using a human-readable duration string (e.g. '3h30m', '1w2d'). Returns updated time stats.

Returns: JSON with time stats including time_estimate, total_time_spent, and human-readable formats. See also: gitlab_mr_time_stats.

name: gitlab_mr_state_event_get
description:
Get a single state event for a merge request.

Returns: JSON with state event details including id, state, user, created_at.

See also: gitlab_mr_state_event_list, gitlab_mr_milestone_event_get

name: gitlab_mr_state_event_list
description:
List state events for a merge request. Shows when the MR was opened, closed, merged, or reopened.

Returns: JSON array of state events with pagination. Fields include id, state, user, created_at.

See also: gitlab_mr_state_event_get, gitlab_mr_label_event_list

name: gitlab_mr_subscribe
description:
Subscribe to a GitLab merge request to receive notifications. Returns the updated MR. Returns 304 if already subscribed.

Returns: JSON with the updated merge request details. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

name: gitlab_mr_time_stats
description:
Get time tracking statistics for a GitLab merge request including estimated time and total time spent in both human-readable and seconds format.

Returns: JSON with time stats including time_estimate, total_time_spent, and human-readable formats. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

name: gitlab_mr_unapprove
description:
Remove the authenticated user's approval from a GitLab merge request.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_approve.

name: gitlab_mr_unsubscribe
description:
Unsubscribe from a GitLab merge request to stop receiving notifications. Returns the updated MR. Returns 304 if not subscribed.

Returns: JSON with the updated merge request details. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

name: gitlab_mr_update
description:
Update a GitLab merge request's title, description, target branch, assignees, reviewers, labels (replace, add, or remove), milestone, discussion_locked, allow_collaboration, or state event (close/reopen). The squash and remove_source_branch options are omitted by default to preserve repository-level settings; only set them when explicitly requested. Only specified fields are changed.

Returns: JSON with the updated merge request details. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_merge.

add_labels.description: Comma-separated label names to add without removing existing

allow_collaboration.description: Allow commits from upstream members who can merge to target branch

assignee_id.description: Single user ID to assign (use assignee_ids for multiple)

assignee_ids.description: User IDs to assign as merge request assignees

discussion_locked.description: Lock discussions on the merge request

labels.description: Comma-separated label names to replace all labels on the merge request

remove_source_branch.description: Delete source branch after merge. Only set if explicitly requested

reviewer_ids.description: User IDs to add as reviewers

squash.description: Squash commits on merge. Only set if explicitly requested

name: gitlab_namespace_exists
description:
Check whether a namespace path exists (is taken). Returns availability and suggested alternatives if the path is taken.

Returns: JSON with namespace availability status.

See also: gitlab_namespace_get, gitlab_namespace_search

id.description: Namespace path to check for existence,required

parent_id.description: Parent namespace ID to scope the check

name: gitlab_namespace_get
description:
Get details of a single namespace by ID or path.

Returns: JSON with namespace details.

See also: gitlab_namespace_list, gitlab_namespace_search

name: gitlab_namespace_list
description:
List all namespaces visible to the authenticated user. Supports filtering by search, owned-only, top-level-only, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of namespaces with pagination.

See also: gitlab_namespace_get, gitlab_list_groups

owned_only.description: If true return only namespaces owned by the authenticated user

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)

top_level_only.description: If true return only top-level namespaces

name: gitlab_namespace_search
description:
Search namespaces by query string. Returns matching namespaces with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching namespaces with pagination.

See also: gitlab_namespace_list, gitlab_namespace_exists

name: gitlab_notification_global_get
description:
Get global notification settings for the authenticated user.

Returns: JSON with global notification settings.

See also: gitlab_notification_global_update, gitlab_notification_project_get

name: gitlab_notification_global_update
description:
Update global notification settings for the authenticated user.

Returns: JSON with the updated global notification settings.

See also: gitlab_notification_global_get, gitlab_notification_project_update

level.description: Notification level: disabled, participating, watch, global, mention, custom

merge_when_pipeline_succeeds.description: Notify on merge when pipeline succeeds

name: gitlab_notification_group_get
description:
Get notification settings for a specific group.

Returns: JSON with group notification settings.

See also: gitlab_notification_group_update, gitlab_notification_global_get

name: gitlab_notification_group_update
description:
Update notification settings for a specific group.

Returns: JSON with the updated group notification settings.

See also: gitlab_notification_group_get, gitlab_notification_global_update

level.description: Notification level: disabled, participating, watch, global, mention, custom

merge_when_pipeline_succeeds.description: Notify on merge when pipeline succeeds

name: gitlab_notification_project_get
description:
Get notification settings for a specific project.

Returns: JSON with project notification settings.

See also: gitlab_notification_project_update, gitlab_notification_global_get

name: gitlab_notification_project_update
description:
Update notification settings for a specific project.

Returns: JSON with the updated project notification settings.

See also: gitlab_notification_project_get, gitlab_notification_global_update

level.description: Notification level: disabled, participating, watch, global, mention, custom

merge_when_pipeline_succeeds.description: Notify on merge when pipeline succeeds

name: gitlab_package_delete
description:
Delete a package and all its files from the GitLab Package Registry. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_package_list, gitlab_package_file_delete

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_package_download
description:
Download a file from the GitLab Generic Package Registry and save it to a local path. Returns the output path, file size, and SHA256 checksum.

See also: gitlab_package_list, gitlab_package_publish

Returns: JSON with output path, file size, and SHA256 checksum.

output_path.description: Absolute path where the file will be saved on the local filesystem,required

name: gitlab_package_file_delete
description:
Delete a single file from a package in the GitLab Package Registry. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_package_file_list, gitlab_package_delete

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_package_file_list
description:
List files within a specific package. Returns file ID, name, size, and SHA256 for each file with pagination.

See also: gitlab_package_list, gitlab_package_download

Returns: JSON array of package files with pagination.

name: gitlab_package_list
description:
List packages in a GitLab project. Can filter by name, version, type, and supports pagination and sorting.

See also: gitlab_package_file_list, gitlab_package_publish

Returns: JSON array of packages with pagination.

order_by.description: Order by: name, created_at, version, type

package_type.description: Filter by type (generic, npm, maven, etc.)

status.description: Filter by status: default, hidden, processing, error

name: gitlab_package_publish
description:
Upload a single file to the GitLab Generic Package Registry. Provide either file_path (absolute local path) or content_base64 (base64-encoded content), not both. Returns the package file ID, size, SHA256, and the real download URL in the 'url' field. Use that 'url' value with gitlab_release_link_create to attach the package to a release — do NOT construct package URLs manually. To upload multiple files at once, use gitlab_package_publish_directory instead. To publish and link to a release in one step, use gitlab_package_publish_and_link.

See also: gitlab_package_publish_and_link, gitlab_package_list

Returns: JSON with the published package details and download URL.

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded file content. Only one should be provided.

file_name.description: Name of the file within the package,required

file_path.description: Absolute path to a local file. Alternative to content_base64. Only one of file_path or content_base64 should be provided.

name: gitlab_package_publish_and_link
description:
Upload a file to the Generic Package Registry and create a release asset link pointing to it in one step (recommended over separate publish + link calls). Provide either file_path or content_base64 for the file content. The release identified by tag_name must already exist. Automatically uses the real download URL — no manual URL construction needed.

IMPORTANT: link_name MUST be the exact filename (e.g. 'checksums.txt.asc'). NEVER add descriptive suffixes like '(GPG signature)' — tools like go-selfupdate match release asset names exactly.

See also: gitlab_package_publish, gitlab_create_release_link

Returns: JSON with the published package details and release link URL.

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded file content. Alternative to file_path.

file_name.description: Name of the file within the package,required

file_path.description: Absolute path to a local file. Alternative to content_base64.

link_name.description: Display name of the release link. Defaults to file_name if omitted. MUST be the exact filename — never add descriptive suffixes.

link_type.description: Type of the release link: package, runbook, image, or other. Defaults to package.

tag_name.description: Tag name of the release to link the package file to,required

name: gitlab_package_publish_directory
description:
Batch-upload all matching files from a local directory to the Generic Package Registry. Walks the directory (non-recursive), filters by an optional glob pattern (e.g. *.tar.gz, *.exe), and publishes each file. Ideal for uploading release binaries or build artifacts in bulk. Returns the list of published files with checksums and URLs.

See also: gitlab_package_publish, gitlab_package_publish_and_link

Returns: JSON with the list of published files, checksums, and URLs.

directory_path.description: Absolute path to a local directory whose files will be published,required

include_pattern.description: Glob pattern to filter files within the directory (e.g. *.tar.gz). If omitted, all regular files are included.

name: gitlab_pages_domain_create
description:
Create a new Pages domain for a project. Optionally configure SSL certificate.

Returns: JSON with the created Pages domain details.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_get, gitlab_pages_domain_delete

key.description: PEM-encoded private key for the certificate

name: gitlab_pages_domain_delete
description:
Delete a Pages domain from a project.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_create

domain.description: The Pages domain name to delete,required

name: gitlab_pages_domain_get
description:
Get a single Pages domain for a project, including certificate details.

Returns: JSON with Pages domain details including SSL certificate information.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_update, gitlab_pages_domain_delete

name: gitlab_pages_domain_list
description:
List Pages domains for a specific project. Supports pagination.

Returns: JSON array of Pages domains with pagination.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_get, gitlab_pages_domain_create

name: gitlab_pages_domain_list_all
description:
List all Pages domains across all projects accessible to the authenticated user.

Returns: JSON array of Pages domains.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_list

name: gitlab_pages_domain_update
description:
Update an existing Pages domain for a project. Can update SSL settings.

Returns: JSON with the updated Pages domain details.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_get

domain.description: The Pages domain name to update,required

key.description: PEM-encoded private key for the certificate

name: gitlab_pages_get
description:
Get Pages settings for a project. Returns URL, unique domain status, HTTPS enforcement, deployments, and primary domain.

Returns: JSON with Pages configuration and deployment details.

See also: gitlab_pages_update, gitlab_pages_unpublish

name: gitlab_pages_unpublish
description:
Unpublish Pages for a project. Removes all published Pages content.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_pages_get

name: gitlab_pages_update
description:
Update Pages settings for a project. Can configure unique domain, HTTPS enforcement, and primary domain.

Returns: JSON with the updated Pages settings.

See also: gitlab_pages_get

name: gitlab_patch_job_token_access_settings
description:
Update the CI/CD job token access settings for a GitLab project.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_get_job_token_access_settings, gitlab_list_job_token_inbound_allowlist

enabled.description: Enable or disable the CI/CD job token scope,required

name: gitlab_personal_access_token_get
description:
Get a personal access token by ID. Use token_id=0 to retrieve the current token used for authentication.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate

Returns: JSON with access token details.

token_id.description: Access token ID (required, use 0 for current token)

name: gitlab_personal_access_token_list
description:
List personal access tokens. Filter by state, search by name, or filter by user ID (admin only).

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_group_access_token_list

Returns: JSON array of access tokens with pagination.

name: gitlab_personal_access_token_revoke
description:
Revoke a personal access token by ID. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_personal_access_token_revoke_self
description:
Revoke the personal access token used for the current request. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_revoke, gitlab_personal_access_token_list

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate
description:
Rotate a personal access token, generating a new token value. Optionally set a new expiry date.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_revoke

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

expires_at.description: New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format

name: gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate_self
description:
Rotate the personal access token used for the current request. Returns the new token value.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate, gitlab_project_access_token_rotate_self

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

expires_at.description: New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format

name: gitlab_pipeline_cancel
description:
Cancel a running pipeline in a GitLab project. Returns the updated pipeline details with canceled status.

Returns: pipeline id, iid, status, ref, sha, web_url, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_retry, gitlab_pipeline_get.

name: gitlab_pipeline_create
description: Create a new pipeline for a branch or tag. Optionally pass variables (key/value pairs with type env_var or file). Returns: id, iid, status, source, ref, sha, web_url, duration, coverage, timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_job_list.

ref.description: Branch or tag name to run the pipeline for

variable_type.description: Variable type (env_var or file, default: env_var)

name: gitlab_pipeline_delete
description:
Permanently delete a pipeline and all its jobs. This action cannot be undone. Requires at least Maintainer access level.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_pipeline_list.

name: gitlab_pipeline_get
description:
Retrieve detailed information about a specific pipeline in a GitLab project. Returns pipeline ID, status, source, ref, SHA, duration, coverage, user, timestamps, and YAML errors. See also: gitlab_job_list, gitlab_pipeline_test_report.

Returns: id, iid, status, source, ref, sha, duration, coverage, user, timestamps, and yaml_errors.

name: gitlab_pipeline_latest
description:
Get the latest pipeline for a project, optionally filtered by branch/tag ref. Returns full pipeline details.

Returns: id, iid, status, source, ref, sha, duration, web_url, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_job_list.

ref.description: Branch or tag name to filter by (defaults to the default branch)

name: gitlab_pipeline_list
description:
List pipelines for a GitLab project. Supports filtering by status (success, failed, running, pending, canceled), scope (running, pending, finished, branches, tags), source (push, web, schedule, merge_request_event), ref (branch/tag), SHA, and username. Returns pipeline ID, status, source, ref, web URL, and timestamps with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of pipelines with id, status, source, ref, sha, web_url, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_job_list.

created_after.description: Return pipelines created after date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)

created_before.description: Return pipelines created before date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)

order_by.description: Order by field (id, status, ref, updated_at, user_id)

ref.description: Filter by branch or tag name

scope.description: Filter by scope (running, pending, finished, branches, tags)

source.description: Filter by source (push, web, trigger, schedule, api, external, pipeline, chat, merge_request_event)

status.description: Filter by status (created, waiting_for_resource, preparing, pending, running, success, failed, canceled, skipped, manual, scheduled)

updated_after.description: Return pipelines updated after date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)

updated_before.description: Return pipelines updated before date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)

username.description: Filter by username that triggered the pipeline

yaml_errors.description: Return only pipelines with YAML errors

name: gitlab_pipeline_retry
description:
Retry only the failed jobs in a pipeline (successful jobs are not re-run). Returns the updated pipeline details. To retry a specific job, use gitlab_job_retry instead.

Returns: pipeline id, iid, status, ref, sha, web_url, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_job_list.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_create
description: Create a new pipeline schedule in a GitLab project. Requires description, ref (branch/tag), and cron expression. Optionally set timezone and active state. Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, next_run_at, active, owner_name. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

active.description: Whether the schedule is active (default: true)

ref.description: Branch or tag to run the pipeline on,required

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_create_variable
description: Create a new variable for a pipeline schedule. Variables are passed to pipelines triggered by the schedule. Supports env_var (default) and file types. Returns: key, value, variable_type. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_delete
description:
Permanently delete a pipeline schedule from a GitLab project. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_delete_variable
description:
Delete a pipeline schedule variable by key. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_edit_variable
description: Edit an existing pipeline schedule variable by key. Updates the value and optionally the variable type. Returns: key, value, variable_type. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get
description:
Get details of a specific pipeline schedule in a GitLab project by its ID. Returns description, ref, cron expression, timezone, active state, owner, and timestamps.

Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, active, next_run_at, owner, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list
description:
List pipeline schedules for a GitLab project. Supports filtering by scope (active, inactive). Returns paginated results with schedule details.

Returns: paginated list of schedules with id, description, ref, cron, active state, and next_run_at. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get, gitlab_pipeline_list.

scope.description: Filter by scope: active or inactive

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list_triggered_pipelines
description:
List all pipelines that were triggered by a specific pipeline schedule. Returns paginated results with pipeline ID, ref, status, and source.

Returns: paginated list of pipelines with id, ref, status, and source. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get, gitlab_pipeline_get.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_run
description:
Trigger an immediate run of a pipeline schedule. Executes the schedule now regardless of its cron timing. Returns the updated schedule details.

Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, active, next_run_at, and owner. See also: gitlab_pipeline_list.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_take_ownership
description:
Take ownership of a pipeline schedule, making the current user the owner. Returns the updated schedule details.

Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, active, next_run_at, and owner. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list.

name: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_update
description: Update an existing pipeline schedule in a GitLab project. All fields are optional: description, ref, cron, timezone, active state. Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, next_run_at, active, owner_name. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

ref.description: Updated branch or tag

name: gitlab_pipeline_security_summary
description: Get security report summary for a pipeline (requires Ultimate/Premium). Returns: scanner-level breakdown (SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, container scanning, secret detection, coverage fuzzing, API fuzzing, cluster image scanning) with vulnerability counts. See also: gitlab_vulnerability_severity_count, gitlab_list_vulnerabilities, gitlab_list_security_findings.

pipeline_iid.description: Pipeline IID (internal ID within the project),required

project_path.description: Full path of the project (e.g. my-group/my-project),required

name: gitlab_pipeline_test_report
description:
Get the full test report for a pipeline. Returns total/passed/failed/skipped/error counts and per-suite breakdowns.

Returns: total, success, failed, skipped, error counts and per-suite test case details. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_job_list.

name: gitlab_pipeline_test_report_summary
description:
Get a summary of the test report for a pipeline. Returns aggregated counts and per-suite summaries with build IDs.

Returns: aggregated test counts and per-suite summaries with build IDs. See also: gitlab_pipeline_test_report.

name: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_create
description:
Create a new pipeline trigger token

Returns: JSON with the created trigger token.

See also: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_list, gitlab_pipeline_trigger_run

name: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_delete
description:
Delete a pipeline trigger token

Returns: JSON confirming trigger deletion.

See also: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_list, gitlab_pipeline_trigger_create

name: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_get
description:
Get a single pipeline trigger token

Returns: JSON with trigger token details.

See also: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_list, gitlab_pipeline_trigger_update

name: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_list
description:
List pipeline trigger tokens for a project

Returns: JSON array of pipeline triggers with pagination.

See also: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_create, gitlab_pipeline_trigger_run

name: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_run
description:
Trigger a pipeline using a trigger token

Returns: JSON with the triggered pipeline details.

See also: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_list, gitlab_create_pipeline

ref.description: Branch or tag name to run pipeline on

variables.description: JSON object of key-value variable pairs

name: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_update
description:
Update a pipeline trigger token description

Returns: JSON with the updated trigger token.

See also: gitlab_pipeline_trigger_get, gitlab_pipeline_trigger_delete

name: gitlab_pipeline_update_metadata
description: Update the display name of an existing pipeline. This is the only field that can be changed — status, ref, variables, and other fields cannot be updated. Returns: id, iid, status, source, ref, sha, web_url, duration. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get.

name: gitlab_pipeline_variables
description:
Get the variables for a specific pipeline. Returns variable keys, values, and types.

Returns: list of variables with key, value, and variable_type. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get.

name: gitlab_pipeline_wait
description:
Wait for a pipeline to reach a terminal state (success, failed, canceled, skipped, manual). Polls the pipeline status at a configurable interval and sends progress notifications. Returns the final pipeline details when done or when the timeout is reached.

Returns: pipeline details, waited_for duration, poll_count, final_status, timed_out flag. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_pipeline_create.

fail_on_error.description: Return isError when pipeline ends in failed/canceled status (default true)

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum wait time in seconds (1-3600, default 300)

name: gitlab_preview_group_badge
description:
Preview how a group badge renders after placeholder interpolation, without creating it.

See also: gitlab_add_group_badge, gitlab_list_group_badges

Returns: JSON with rendered badge URLs.

name: gitlab_preview_project_badge
description:
Preview how a project badge renders after placeholder interpolation, without creating it.

See also: gitlab_add_project_badge, gitlab_list_project_badges

Returns: JSON with rendered badge URLs.

name: gitlab_project_access_token_create
description:
Create a new project access token with specified name, scopes, access level, and optional expiry date.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_group_access_token_create

Returns: JSON with the created access token including the token value.

scopes.description: Token scopes: api, read_api, read_repository, write_repository, etc.,required

name: gitlab_project_access_token_get
description:
Get a specific project access token by its ID.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_project_access_token_rotate

Returns: JSON with access token details.

name: gitlab_project_access_token_list
description:
List all access tokens for a GitLab project. Filter by state (active, inactive).

See also: gitlab_group_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_list

Returns: JSON array of access tokens with pagination.

name: gitlab_project_access_token_revoke
description:
Revoke a project access token. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_project_access_token_create

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_project_access_token_rotate
description:
Rotate a project access token, generating a new token value. Optionally set a new expiry date.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_project_access_token_revoke

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

expires_at.description: New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format

name: gitlab_project_access_token_rotate_self
description:
Rotate the project access token used for the current request. Returns the new token value.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_rotate, gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate_self

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

expires_at.description: New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format

name: gitlab_project_add_push_rule
description:
Add push rule configuration to a project. Enforce commit message format, branch naming, file size limits, secret detection, and signed commits.

Returns: JSON with the created push rule configuration.

See also: gitlab_project_get_push_rules.

author_email_regex.description: Regex to validate author email addresses

commit_committer_check.description: Reject commits where committer is not a project member

commit_committer_name_check.description: Reject commits where committer name does not match user name

commit_message_negative_regex.description: Regex that commit messages must NOT match

commit_message_regex.description: Regex that commit messages must match

member_check.description: Only allow commits from project members

prevent_secrets.description: Reject files that are likely to contain secrets

reject_unsigned_commits.description: Reject commits that are not GPG signed

name: gitlab_project_approval_config_change
description:
Update the project-level merge request approval configuration. Only specified fields are modified.

Returns: JSON with updated approval settings.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_config_get.

approvals_before_merge.description: Number of approvals required before merge

merge_requests_author_approval.description: Allow MR author to approve their own MR

reset_approvals_on_push.description: Reset approvals when new commits are pushed

selective_code_owner_removals.description: Only remove code owner approvals when relevant files change

name: gitlab_project_approval_config_get
description:
Get the project-level merge request approval configuration including required approvals, author self-approval, committer approval, and password requirements.

Returns: JSON with approval settings.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_config_change, gitlab_project_approval_rule_list.

name: gitlab_project_approval_rule_create
description:
Create a new project-level approval rule with the specified number of required approvals and approver groups/users.

Returns: JSON with the created approval rule details.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_list, gitlab_project_approval_rule_update.

applies_to_all_protected_branches.description: Apply this rule to all protected branches

group_ids.description: Group IDs to assign as approvers

protected_branch_ids.description: Protected branch IDs to scope the rule to

user_ids.description: User IDs to assign as approvers

name: gitlab_project_approval_rule_delete
description:
Delete a project-level approval rule.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_list, gitlab_project_approval_rule_create.

name: gitlab_project_approval_rule_get
description:
Get details of a specific project-level approval rule.

Returns: JSON with approval rule details including name, approvals_required, eligible approvers, and protected branches.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_list, gitlab_project_approval_rule_update.

name: gitlab_project_approval_rule_list
description:
List project-level approval rules. Each rule specifies the number of required approvals and eligible approvers.

Returns: JSON array of approval rules with pagination.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_get, gitlab_project_approval_rule_create.

name: gitlab_project_approval_rule_update
description:
Update an existing project-level approval rule. Only specified fields are modified.

Returns: JSON with the updated approval rule details.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_get, gitlab_project_approval_rule_delete.

applies_to_all_protected_branches.description: Apply this rule to all protected branches

group_ids.description: Updated group IDs to assign as approvers

user_ids.description: Updated user IDs to assign as approvers

usernames.description: Updated usernames to assign as approvers

name: gitlab_project_archive
description:
Archive a GitLab project, making it read-only. Archived projects are hidden from default project list.

Returns: JSON with updated project details. See also: gitlab_project_unarchive, gitlab_project_get.

name: gitlab_project_create
description:
Create a new GitLab project (repository). Supports setting namespace, visibility (private/internal/public), description, default branch, optional README initialization, merge method, squash option, topics, and feature flags (issues_enabled, merge_requests_enabled, wiki_enabled, jobs_enabled, lfs_enabled, request_access_enabled). Also supports CI/CD config path, allow_merge_on_skipped_pipeline, remove_source_branch_after_merge, and autoclose_referenced_issues.

Returns: JSON with the created project details including id, name, path_with_namespace, web_url, visibility, and namespace. See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_project_list.

allow_merge_on_skipped_pipeline.description: Allow merge when pipeline is skipped

container_registry_access_level.description: Container registry access level (disabled, private, enabled)

only_allow_merge_if_all_discussions_are_.description: Only allow merge when all discussions are resolved

only_allow_merge_if_pipeline_succeeds.description: Only allow merge when pipeline succeeds

package_registry_access_level.description: Package registry access level (disabled, private, enabled)

pages_access_level.description: Pages access level (disabled, private, enabled, public)

remove_source_branch_after_merge.description: Remove source branch after merge by default

name: gitlab_project_create_for_user
description:
Create a new project owned by the specified user (admin operation). The project is created in the target user's personal namespace by default.

Returns: JSON with the created project details.

See also: gitlab_project_create, gitlab_project_get.

namespace_id.description: Namespace ID (defaults to user personal namespace)

user_id.description: Target user ID who will own the project,required

name: gitlab_project_create_fork_relation
description:
Create a fork relation between two existing projects. This makes the project appear as forked from the specified source project.

Returns: JSON with fork relation details including forked_to_project_id and forked_from_project_id.

See also: gitlab_project_fork, gitlab_project_delete_fork_relation.

forked_from_id.description: ID of the project to set as the fork source,required

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path of the forked project,required

name: gitlab_project_delete
description:
Delete a GitLab project. On instances with delayed deletion enabled, the project is marked for deletion (scheduled) rather than removed immediately — the response includes the scheduled deletion date. Set permanently_remove=true with full_path to bypass delayed deletion and permanently remove the project immediately (requires admin on some instances). Use gitlab_project_restore to cancel a scheduled deletion. Use gitlab_project_list with include_pending_delete=true to see projects awaiting deletion.

Returns: confirmation message with scheduled deletion date if delayed deletion is enabled. See also: gitlab_project_create, gitlab_project_list.

full_path.description: Full path of the project to confirm permanent removal (required when permanently_remove is true)

permanently_remove.description: If true, immediately and permanently delete the project bypassing delayed deletion. Requires admin permissions on some GitLab instances.

name: gitlab_project_delete_fork_relation
description:
Remove the fork relation from a project. The project will no longer be marked as a fork.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_create_fork_relation.

name: gitlab_project_delete_push_rule
description:
Delete the push rule configuration from a project. This removes all push restrictions (commit format, branch naming, file size, etc.).

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_get_push_rules.

name: gitlab_project_delete_shared_group
description:
Remove a shared group from a project, revoking the group's access.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_project_share_with_group.

group_id.description: Group ID to remove from project sharing,required

name: gitlab_project_download_avatar
description:
Download the avatar image for a project as base64-encoded data.

Returns: JSON with content_base64 (base64-encoded image) and size_bytes.

See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_project_upload_avatar.

name: gitlab_project_edit_push_rule
description:
Modify the push rule configuration of a project. Update commit message, branch name, file restrictions, or signing requirements.

Returns: JSON with the updated push rule configuration.

See also: gitlab_project_get_push_rules.

author_email_regex.description: Regex to validate author email addresses

commit_committer_check.description: Reject commits where committer is not a project member

commit_committer_name_check.description: Reject commits where committer name does not match user name

commit_message_negative_regex.description: Regex that commit messages must NOT match

commit_message_regex.description: Regex that commit messages must match

member_check.description: Only allow commits from project members

prevent_secrets.description: Reject files that are likely to contain secrets

reject_unsigned_commits.description: Reject commits that are not GPG signed

name: gitlab_project_event_list
description:
List all visible events for a project. Supports filtering by action type, target type, date range, sort order, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of events with pagination.

See also: gitlab_user_contribution_event_list, gitlab_list_commits

action.description: Filter by action type (created, updated, closed, reopened, pushed, commented, merged, joined, left, destroyed, expired)

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)

sort.description: Sort order (asc or desc, default desc)

target_type.description: Filter by target type (issue, milestone, merge_request, note, project, snippet, user)

name: gitlab_project_fork
description:
Fork a GitLab project into a new project. Optionally specify target namespace (namespace_id or namespace_path), name, path, description, visibility, branches to include, and whether MR default target should be the fork itself (mr_default_target_self).

Returns: JSON with the forked project details including id, name, web_url, and forked_from_project. See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_branch_create.

mr_default_target_self.description: MR default target is the fork itself instead of upstream

path.description: Path slug for the forked project

project_id.description: Source project ID or URL-encoded path,required

name: gitlab_project_get
description:
Retrieve detailed metadata for a GitLab project including name, description, visibility, web URL, default branch, and namespace. Accepts numeric project ID or URL-encoded path (e.g. 'group/subgroup/project'). The response includes default_branch which MUST be used instead of assuming 'main' when generating repository file URLs. Set statistics=true to include repository statistics, license=true to include license info, with_custom_attributes=true to include custom attributes.

Returns: JSON with project details including id, name, description, visibility, web_url, default_branch, namespace, and statistics (if requested). See also: gitlab_project_update, gitlab_project_list.

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path (e.g. 'user/repo' or 42),required

statistics.description: Include project statistics (commit count, storage sizes)

name: gitlab_project_get_push_rules
description:
Get the push rule configuration for a project (commit message, branch name, file size restrictions, etc.).

Returns: JSON with push rule configuration including commit_message_regex, branch_name_regex, max_file_size, and signing requirements.

See also: gitlab_project_get.

name: gitlab_project_hook_add
description:
Add a webhook to a GitLab project. Configure the URL, secret token, SSL verification, and which events trigger the webhook (push, issues, MRs, tags, notes, jobs, pipelines, wiki, deployments, releases, emoji, etc.).

Returns: JSON with the created webhook details including id, url, and event settings. See also: gitlab_project_hook_list, gitlab_project_hook_edit.

resource_access_token_events.description: Trigger on resource access token events

name: gitlab_project_hook_delete
description:
Delete a webhook from a GitLab project.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_project_hook_list, gitlab_project_hook_add.

name: gitlab_project_hook_delete_custom_header
description:
Delete a custom header from a project webhook.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_hook_get, gitlab_project_hook_set_custom_header.

key.description: Custom header key name to delete,required

name: gitlab_project_hook_delete_url_variable
description:
Delete a URL variable from a project webhook.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_hook_get, gitlab_project_hook_set_url_variable.

key.description: URL variable key name to delete,required

name: gitlab_project_hook_edit
description:
Edit an existing project webhook. Update the URL, events, SSL verification, secret token, or other settings.

Returns: JSON with the updated webhook details. See also: gitlab_project_hook_get, gitlab_project_hook_delete.

resource_access_token_events.description: Trigger on resource access token events

name: gitlab_project_hook_get
description:
Get details of a specific project webhook including all event trigger settings.

Returns: JSON with webhook details including id, url, created_at, and all event trigger settings. See also: gitlab_project_hook_list, gitlab_project_hook_edit.

name: gitlab_project_hook_list
description:
List webhooks configured for a GitLab project.

Returns: JSON array of webhooks with pagination. Fields include id, url, created_at, and event trigger settings. See also: gitlab_project_hook_get, gitlab_project_hook_add.

name: gitlab_project_hook_set_custom_header
description:
Set a custom header on a project webhook. The header will be included in all webhook requests. If the header already exists, its value will be updated.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_hook_get, gitlab_project_hook_delete_custom_header.

name: gitlab_project_hook_set_url_variable
description:
Set a URL variable on a project webhook. URL variables can be used in the webhook URL as {variable_name}. If already exists, its value will be updated.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_hook_get, gitlab_project_hook_delete_url_variable.

name: gitlab_project_hook_test
description:
Trigger a test event for a project webhook. Sends a sample payload for the specified event type (push_events, issues_events, merge_requests_events, etc.).

Returns: confirmation message indicating the test event was sent. See also: gitlab_project_hook_get, gitlab_project_hook_list.

event.description: Event type to trigger (push_events, issues_events, merge_requests_events, tag_push_events, note_events, job_events, pipeline_events, wiki_page_events, releases_events, emoji_events),required

name: gitlab_project_invite
description:
Invite a user to a project by email or user ID. Requires access_level (10=Guest, 20=Reporter, 30=Developer, 40=Maintainer, 50=Owner).

Returns: JSON with the invitation result.

See also: gitlab_project_invite_list_pending, gitlab_add_project_member

email.description: Email address to invite (either email or user_id required)

user_id.description: User ID to invite (either email or user_id required)

name: gitlab_project_invite_list_pending
description:
List all pending invitations for a project. Supports filtering by query and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of pending invitations with pagination.

See also: gitlab_project_invite, gitlab_list_project_members

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)

query.description: Filter invitations by email or name

name: gitlab_project_languages
description:
List programming languages used in a GitLab project with their percentages.

Returns: JSON object mapping language names to their percentage of the codebase. See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_repository_tree.

name: gitlab_project_list
description:
List GitLab projects accessible to the authenticated user. Supports filtering by ownership, search term, visibility, archived status, topic, minimum access level, starred, membership, last_activity_after/before dates, and feature flags (with_issues_enabled, with_merge_requests_enabled). Set include_pending_delete=true to include projects that are marked/scheduled for deletion (hidden by default). Supports ordering, sorting, simple mode, search_namespaces, and statistics.

Returns: JSON array of projects with pagination. Fields include id, name, path_with_namespace, visibility, web_url, and default_branch. See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_project_create.

archived.description: Filter by archived status (true=only archived, false=only active)

id_after.description: Return projects with ID greater than this value (keyset pagination)

id_before.description: Return projects with ID less than this value (keyset pagination)

include_pending_delete.description: Include projects that are marked/scheduled for deletion. Default false.

last_activity_after.description: Return projects with last activity after date (ISO 8601 format)

last_activity_before.description: Return projects with last activity before date (ISO 8601 format)

membership.description: Limit to projects where current user is a member

order_by.description: Order by field (id, name, path, created_at, updated_at, last_activity_at)

owned.description: Limit to projects explicitly owned by the current user

simple.description: Return only limited fields (faster for large result sets)

starred.description: Limit to projects starred by the current user

with_issues_enabled.description: Filter by projects with issues feature enabled

with_merge_requests_enabled.description: Filter by projects with merge requests enabled

name: gitlab_project_list_forks
description:
List forks of a GitLab project. Supports filtering by ownership, search, visibility, ordering, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of forked projects with pagination. Fields include id, name, path_with_namespace, and web_url. See also: gitlab_project_fork, gitlab_project_get.

order_by.description: Order by field (id, name, path, created_at, updated_at, last_activity_at)

owned.description: Limit to forks owned by the current user

name: gitlab_project_list_groups
description:
List ancestor groups of a project. Supports filtering by search, shared groups, minimum access level, skip_groups, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of groups with pagination. Fields include id, name, path, and visibility.

See also: gitlab_project_get.

shared_visible_only.description: Only show shared groups visible to the current user

skip_groups.description: Array of group IDs to exclude

name: gitlab_project_list_invited_groups
description:
List groups that have been invited/shared to a project. Supports filtering by search, minimum access level, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of invited groups with pagination. Fields include id, name, path, and visibility.

See also: gitlab_project_list_groups.

name: gitlab_project_list_starrers
description:
List users who have starred a project. Supports filtering by search (name or username) and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of users who starred the project with pagination. Fields include starred_since and user details.

See also: gitlab_project_get.

name: gitlab_project_list_user_contributed
description:
List projects that a specific user has contributed to. Supports filtering by search, visibility, archived status, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of projects with pagination. Fields include id, name, path_with_namespace, visibility, and web_url.

See also: gitlab_project_list.

order_by.description: Order by field (id, name, path, created_at, updated_at, last_activity_at)

name: gitlab_project_list_user_projects
description:
List projects owned by a specific user. Accepts user ID or username. Supports filtering by search, visibility, archived status, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of projects with pagination. Fields include id, name, path_with_namespace, visibility, and web_url.

See also: gitlab_project_list.

order_by.description: Order by field (id, name, path, created_at, updated_at, last_activity_at)

name: gitlab_project_list_user_starred
description:
List projects that a specific user has starred. Supports filtering by search, visibility, archived status, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of projects with pagination. Fields include id, name, path_with_namespace, visibility, and web_url.

See also: gitlab_project_list.

order_by.description: Order by field (id, name, path, created_at, updated_at, last_activity_at)

name: gitlab_project_list_users
description:
List users who are members of a project. Supports filtering by search (name or username) and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of project users with pagination. Fields include id, username, name, and state.

See also: gitlab_project_member_list.

name: gitlab_project_member_add
description: Add a user as a project member. Requires user_id (from gitlab_search_users or gitlab_project_members_list) and access_level (10=Guest, 20=Reporter, 30=Developer, 40=Maintainer, 50=Owner). Optionally set expires_at and member_role_id. Returns: username, access level, state, and web URL. See also: gitlab_project_members_list, gitlab_get_user.

user_id.description: User ID to add (provide user_id or username),required

username.description: Username to add (provide user_id or username)

name: gitlab_project_member_delete
description:
Remove a member from a project.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_member_list.

user_id.description: User ID of the member to remove,required

name: gitlab_project_member_edit
description: Edit a project member's access level or expiration. Requires access_level (10=Guest, 20=Reporter, 30=Developer, 40=Maintainer, 50=Owner). Returns: updated username, access level, state, and web URL. See also: gitlab_project_member_get.

user_id.description: User ID of the member to edit,required

name: gitlab_project_member_get
description:
Get details of a specific project member by user ID. Returns access level, state, username, and membership info.

Returns: JSON with member details. See also: gitlab_project_members_list.

name: gitlab_project_member_get_inherited
description:
Get a project member including inherited membership from parent groups. Returns access level, state, and membership origin.

Returns: JSON with member details. See also: gitlab_project_member_get.

name: gitlab_project_members_list
description:
List all members of a GitLab project including inherited members from parent groups. Returns user ID, username, name, state, access level (10=Guest, 20=Reporter, 30=Developer, 40=Maintainer, 50=Owner), and web URL. Supports filtering by name/username query.

Returns: JSON array of members with pagination. See also: gitlab_project_member_get, gitlab_project_get.

query.description: Filter members by name or username

name: gitlab_project_pull_mirror_configure
description:
Configure or update pull mirroring for a project. Set the mirror URL, authentication, branch filtering, and trigger options.

Returns: JSON with updated pull mirror configuration.

See also: gitlab_project_pull_mirror_get, gitlab_project_start_mirroring.

auth_password.description: Authentication password for the mirror URL

auth_user.description: Authentication username for the mirror URL

mirror_branch_regex.description: Regex to filter branches to mirror

mirror_overwrites_diverged_branches.description: Overwrite diverged branches on mirror update

mirror_trigger_builds.description: Trigger CI builds when mirror updates

name: gitlab_project_pull_mirror_get
description:
Get pull mirror configuration for a project including URL, status, last update times, and mirror settings.

Returns: JSON with pull mirror details.

See also: gitlab_project_pull_mirror_configure, gitlab_project_start_mirroring.

name: gitlab_project_repository_storage_get
description:
Get repository storage information for a project including disk path and storage name.

Returns: JSON with project_id, disk_path, repository_storage, and created_at.

See also: gitlab_project_get.

name: gitlab_project_restore
description:
Restore a GitLab project that has been marked/scheduled for deletion. Use gitlab_project_list with include_pending_delete=true to discover projects pending deletion.

Returns: JSON with the restored project details including id, name, web_url, and visibility.

See also: gitlab_project_get.

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path of the project marked for deletion,required

name: gitlab_project_share_with_group
description:
Share a project with a group, granting the specified access level. Optionally set an expiration date (YYYY-MM-DD). Access levels: 10=Guest, 20=Reporter, 30=Developer, 40=Maintainer.

Returns: JSON with the group share details including group_id, group_access, and expires_at. See also: gitlab_project_delete_shared_group, gitlab_group_list.

name: gitlab_project_snippet_content
description:
Get the raw content of a project snippet.

Returns: raw snippet content. See also: gitlab_project_snippet_get.

name: gitlab_project_snippet_create
description:
Create a new snippet in a GitLab project. Use 'files' for multi-file snippets or 'file_name'+'content' for single-file.

Returns: JSON with the created snippet details. See also: gitlab_project_snippet_get.

content.description: Content (single-file, deprecated in favor of files)

file_name.description: File name (single-file, deprecated in favor of files)

files.description: Files to include in the snippet

file_path.description: File path for the snippet file,required

name: gitlab_project_snippet_delete
description:
Delete a project snippet. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_project_snippet_list.

name: gitlab_project_snippet_get
description:
Get a single project snippet by ID.

Returns: JSON with snippet details. See also: gitlab_project_snippet_update.

name: gitlab_project_snippet_list
description:
List snippets for a GitLab project.

Returns: JSON array of snippets with pagination. See also: gitlab_project_snippet_get, gitlab_project_snippet_create.

name: gitlab_project_snippet_update
description:
Update an existing project snippet.

Returns: JSON with the updated snippet details. See also: gitlab_project_snippet_get.

content.description: New content (single-file, deprecated in favor of files)

file_name.description: New file name (single-file, deprecated in favor of files)

action.description: File action: create, update, delete, move,required

file_path.description: File path,required

visibility.description: New visibility: private, internal, or public

name: gitlab_project_star
description:
Star a GitLab project for the authenticated user.

Returns: JSON with updated project details including incremented star_count. See also: gitlab_project_get.

name: gitlab_project_start_housekeeping
description:
Trigger housekeeping for a project (git gc, repack, and other repository optimization tasks).

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_project_repository_storage_get.

name: gitlab_project_start_mirroring
description:
Trigger an immediate pull mirror update for a project. The project must have pull mirroring configured.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_pull_mirror_get, gitlab_project_pull_mirror_configure.

name: gitlab_project_transfer
description:
