Transfer a GitLab project to a different namespace. Requires the namespace (ID or path) to transfer to.

Returns: JSON with updated project details including new path and namespace. See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_group_list.

name: gitlab_project_unarchive
description:
Unarchive a GitLab project, restoring it from read-only state.

Returns: JSON with updated project details. See also: gitlab_project_archive, gitlab_project_get.

name: gitlab_project_unstar
description:
Remove star from a GitLab project for the authenticated user.

Returns: JSON with updated project details including decremented star_count. See also: gitlab_project_get.

name: gitlab_project_update
description:
Update GitLab project settings such as name, description, visibility, default branch, merge method, squash option, topics, and feature flags (issues_enabled, merge_requests_enabled, wiki_enabled, jobs_enabled, lfs_enabled). Also supports CI/CD config path, merge commit/squash commit templates, merge_pipelines_enabled, merge_trains_enabled, allow_merge_on_skipped_pipeline, remove_source_branch_after_merge, autoclose_referenced_issues, resolve_outdated_diff_discussions, and approvals_before_merge. Only specified fields are modified; unset fields remain unchanged.

Returns: JSON with the updated project details. See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_project_delete.

allow_merge_on_skipped_pipeline.description: Allow merge when pipeline is skipped

approvals_before_merge.description: Number of approvals required before merge

container_registry_access_level.description: Container registry access level (disabled, private, enabled)

merge_request_title_regex.description: Regex that MR titles must match

merge_request_title_regex_description.description: Human-readable description for the MR title regex

only_allow_merge_if_all_discussions_are_.description: Only allow merge when all discussions are resolved

only_allow_merge_if_pipeline_succeeds.description: Only allow merge when pipeline succeeds

package_registry_access_level.description: Package registry access level (disabled, private, enabled)

pages_access_level.description: Pages access level (disabled, private, enabled, public)

remove_source_branch_after_merge.description: Remove source branch after merge by default

name: gitlab_project_upload
description:
Upload a file to a GitLab project's markdown uploads area. Provide either file_path (absolute path to a local file) or content_base64 (base64-encoded content), not both. Returns a Markdown embed string (e.g. '![alt](/uploads/hash/filename)') that can be inserted into MR descriptions, note bodies, or discussion bodies.

Returns: JSON with the upload URL and Markdown embed string.

See also: gitlab_project_upload_list, gitlab_project_upload_delete

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded file content. Only one of file_path or content_base64 should be provided.

file_path.description: Absolute path to a local file on the MCP server filesystem. Alternative to content_base64 for files too large to base64-encode. Only one of file_path or content_base64 should be provided.

filename.description: Name of the file to upload (e.g. screenshot.png),required

name: gitlab_project_upload_avatar
description:
Upload or replace the avatar image for a project. Provide either file_path (absolute path to a local image file) or content_base64 (base64-encoded image content), not both.

Returns: JSON with updated project details including avatar_url.

See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_project_download_avatar.

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded image content. Only one of file_path or content_base64 should be provided.

file_path.description: Absolute path to a local image file on the MCP server filesystem. Alternative to content_base64 for files too large to base64-encode. Only one of file_path or content_base64 should be provided.

name: gitlab_project_upload_delete
description:
Delete a file upload (markdown attachment) from a GitLab project by upload ID. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_upload_list

upload_id.description: ID of the upload to delete,required

name: gitlab_project_upload_list
description:
List all file uploads (markdown attachments) for a GitLab project. Returns upload ID, filename, size, and creation date for each upload.

Returns: JSON array of uploads with pagination.

See also: gitlab_project_upload

name: gitlab_protected_branch_get
description: Get details of a single protected branch by name. Returns: branch name, push/merge access levels, allow_force_push, and code_owner_approval_required. See also: gitlab_protected_branches_list, gitlab_branch_protect.

name: gitlab_protected_branch_update
description:
Update settings on an existing protected branch (allow_force_push, code_owner_approval_required). Use gitlab_branch_protect to initially protect a branch. Returns: updated name, push/merge access levels, allow_force_push, and code_owner_approval_required.

See also: gitlab_protected_branch_get, gitlab_protected_branches_list.

allow_force_push.description: Allow force push to this branch

name: gitlab_protected_branches_list
description:
List all protected branches in a GitLab project with their configured push and merge access level restrictions. Returns paginated results.

Returns: paginated list of protected branches with name, push/merge access levels, and allow_force_push. See also: gitlab_branch_protect, gitlab_protected_branch_get.

name: gitlab_protected_environment_get
description:
Get a single protected environment by name, including deploy access levels and approval rules.

See also: gitlab_protected_environment_list, gitlab_get_environment

Returns: JSON with protected environment details (name, deploy access levels, approval rules).

name: gitlab_protected_environment_list
description:
List protected environments in a GitLab project with their deploy access levels and approval rules.

See also: gitlab_protected_environment_protect, gitlab_list_environments

Returns: JSON with array of protected environments and pagination info.

name: gitlab_protected_environment_protect
description:
Protect an environment in a GitLab project. Configure deploy access levels, required approvals, and approval rules.

See also: gitlab_protected_environment_list, gitlab_create_environment

Returns: JSON with the newly protected environment details.

name: gitlab_protected_environment_unprotect
description:
Remove protection from an environment. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_protected_environment_list, gitlab_protected_environment_protect

Returns: JSON confirmation of unprotection.

name: gitlab_protected_environment_update
description:
Update a protected environment's deploy access levels, approval rules, or required approval count.

See also: gitlab_protected_environment_get, gitlab_protected_environment_protect

Returns: JSON with the updated protected environment details.

_destroy.description: Set true to remove this rule

id.description: Existing approval rule ID to update

_destroy.description: Set true to remove this access level

id.description: Existing access level ID to update

name: gitlab_purge_dependency_proxy
description:
Purge the dependency proxy cache for a GitLab group.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_group_get.

name: gitlab_read_repository_submodule_file
description:
Read a file from inside a Git submodule transparently. Resolves the submodule's target project and pinned commit SHA, then fetches the file content. No need to manually find the submodule's project or commit.

Returns: JSON with the file content.

See also: gitlab_list_repository_submodules, gitlab_file_get

file_path.description: Path of the file inside the submodule (e.g. src/main.c),required

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path of the parent repository,required

ref.description: Branch/tag/SHA in the parent repository (defaults to default branch)

submodule_path.description: Path to the submodule as defined in .gitmodules (e.g. libs/core-module),required

name: gitlab_register_cluster_agent
description:
Register a new cluster agent for a GitLab project

See also: gitlab_list_cluster_agents, gitlab_create_cluster_agent_token

Returns: JSON with the registered agent details.

name: gitlab_registry_delete_repository
description:
Delete a container registry repository. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_registry_list_project, gitlab_registry_get_repository

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_registry_delete_tag
description:
Delete a single container registry repository tag. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_registry_list_tags, gitlab_registry_delete_tags_bulk

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_registry_delete_tags_bulk
description:
Delete container registry repository tags in bulk using regex patterns. Use name_regex_delete to match tags to delete and name_regex_keep to exclude tags from deletion.

See also: gitlab_registry_list_tags, gitlab_registry_delete_tag

Returns: confirmation message.

keep_n.description: Number of latest tags to keep

name_regex_delete.description: Regex pattern of tag names to delete

name_regex_keep.description: Regex pattern of tag names to keep

name: gitlab_registry_get_repository
description:
Get details of a single container registry repository by its ID.

See also: gitlab_registry_list_project, gitlab_registry_list_tags

Returns: JSON with repository details.

name: gitlab_registry_get_tag
description:
Get details of a specific container registry repository tag.

See also: gitlab_registry_list_tags, gitlab_registry_delete_tag

Returns: JSON with tag details.

name: gitlab_registry_list_group
description:
List container registry repositories for a GitLab group.

See also: gitlab_registry_list_project, gitlab_registry_get_repository

Returns: JSON array of container repositories with pagination.

name: gitlab_registry_list_project
description:
List container registry repositories for a GitLab project.

See also: gitlab_registry_list_tags, gitlab_registry_list_group

Returns: JSON array of container repositories with pagination.

name: gitlab_registry_list_tags
description:
List tags for a container registry repository.

See also: gitlab_registry_get_tag, gitlab_registry_get_repository

Returns: JSON array of tags with pagination.

name: gitlab_registry_protection_create
description:
Create a container registry protection rule to restrict push/delete access by minimum access level.

See also: gitlab_registry_protection_list, gitlab_registry_list_project

Returns: JSON with the created protection rule details.

minimum_access_level_for_delete.description: Minimum access level for delete (maintainer, owner, admin)

minimum_access_level_for_push.description: Minimum access level for push (maintainer, owner, admin)

name: gitlab_registry_protection_delete
description:
Delete a container registry protection rule. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_registry_protection_list, gitlab_registry_protection_create

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_registry_protection_list
description:
List container registry protection rules for a GitLab project.

See also: gitlab_registry_protection_create, gitlab_registry_list_project

Returns: JSON array of protection rules.

name: gitlab_registry_protection_update
description:
Update a container registry protection rule.

See also: gitlab_registry_protection_list, gitlab_registry_protection_create

Returns: JSON with the updated protection rule details.

minimum_access_level_for_delete.description: Minimum access level for delete (maintainer, owner, admin)

minimum_access_level_for_push.description: Minimum access level for push (maintainer, owner, admin)

name: gitlab_reject_user
description:
Reject a pending GitLab user registration (admin only). The user account will be deleted.

See also: gitlab_approve_user, gitlab_list_users

Returns: JSON with action confirmation.

name: gitlab_release_create
description:
Create a GitLab release. If the tag_name does not exist yet, provide 'ref' (branch name or commit SHA) and GitLab will auto-create a lightweight tag — no need to call gitlab_tag_create first. Use 'tag_message' for an annotated tag. The response includes 'assets_sources' with auto-generated tar.gz and zip archive URLs — use those real URLs instead of constructing download links manually.

Returns: tag_name, name, description, author, commit, created_at, released_at, assets_sources, and assets_links. See also: gitlab_tag_create, gitlab_release_link_create.

milestones.description: Milestone titles to associate with the release

ref.description: Branch or commit SHA to create tag from (if tag does not exist)

released_at.description: Date of the release in ISO 8601 format

tag_message.description: Message to use for the annotated tag (creates annotated tag instead of lightweight)

tag_name.description: Tag name associated with the release,required

name: gitlab_release_delete
description:
Delete a GitLab release. The underlying Git tag is preserved and not deleted.

Returns: deleted release details with tag_name, name, and description. See also: gitlab_release_list, gitlab_tag_delete.

tag_name.description: Tag name of the release to delete,required

name: gitlab_release_get
description:
Retrieve detailed information about a specific GitLab release by its tag name, including title, description, author, creation date, and associated assets. The response includes 'assets_sources' (auto-generated tar.gz/zip archive URLs) and 'assets_links' (manually added links such as package download URLs).

Returns: tag_name, name, description, author, commit, created_at, released_at, assets_sources, and assets_links. See also: gitlab_release_update, gitlab_release_link_list.

name: gitlab_release_latest
description:
Get the latest release for a GitLab project. Returns the most recently created release without needing to know the tag name.

Returns: tag_name, name, description, author, commit, created_at, released_at, and assets. See also: gitlab_release_list, gitlab_release_get.

name: gitlab_release_link_create
description:
Add an asset link to a GitLab release. IMPORTANT: (1) When linking to uploaded packages, use the real 'url' value returned by gitlab_package_publish — do NOT construct package URLs manually. (2) The 'name' MUST be the exact filename (e.g. 'checksums.txt.asc'), NEVER add descriptive suffixes. Consider using gitlab_package_publish_and_link instead to upload and link in one step. Supports link types: runbook, package, image, or other. Links appear in the release's assets section.

Returns: JSON with the created release link (ID, name, URL, type). See also: gitlab_release_get.

link_type.description: Type of the link (runbook, package, image, other)

url.description: URL of the link target. For packages use the real url returned by gitlab_package_publish — never construct URLs manually,required

name: gitlab_release_link_create_batch
description:
Add multiple asset links to a GitLab release in a single call. Use this instead of calling gitlab_release_link_create multiple times. Each link requires a name and url; link_type is optional (runbook, package, image, other). IMPORTANT: (1) For package links, use the real 'url' values returned by gitlab_package_publish. (2) Link names MUST be exact filenames — never add descriptive suffixes.

Returns: JSON with arrays of created links and any failures. See also: gitlab_release_link_list.

links.description: Array of links to create (each with name and url required),required

link_type.description: Type of the link (runbook, package, image, other)

name: gitlab_release_link_delete
description:
Remove an asset link from a GitLab release by its link ID.

Returns: JSON with the deleted release link details. See also: gitlab_release_link_list.

link_id.description: ID of the release link to delete,required

name: gitlab_release_link_get
description:
Get details of a specific release asset link by its ID, including name, URL, type, and whether it is external.

Returns: JSON with release link details (ID, name, URL, type, external flag). See also: gitlab_release_link_list.

name: gitlab_release_link_list
description:
List all asset links attached to a specific GitLab release identified by tag name. Returns link names, URLs, types, and IDs.

Returns: JSON with array of release links and pagination info. See also: gitlab_release_get, gitlab_release_link_get.

name: gitlab_release_link_update
description:
Update an existing release asset link. Can change name, URL, filepath, direct asset path, or link type. Only specified fields are changed.

Returns: JSON with the updated release link details. See also: gitlab_release_link_get.

direct_asset_path.description: New direct asset path for the link

filepath.description: New filepath for a direct asset link

link_id.description: ID of the release link to update,required

link_type.description: New link type (runbook, package, image, other)

name: gitlab_release_list
description:
List all releases for a GitLab project ordered by release date. Returns paginated results including each release's metadata, tag, and asset links.

Returns: paginated list of releases with tag_name, name, description, author, and assets. See also: gitlab_release_get, gitlab_tag_list.

order_by.description: Order by field (released_at, created_at)

name: gitlab_release_update
description: Update an existing GitLab release's title, description, milestones, or released date. Identified by project and tag name. Only specified fields are changed. Returns: tag_name, name, description, author, dates, commit SHA, milestones, and asset links. See also: gitlab_release_get, gitlab_release_delete.

milestones.description: Milestone titles to associate with the release

released_at.description: New release date in ISO 8601 format

name: gitlab_remove_group_job_token_allowlist
description:
Remove a group from the CI/CD job token allowlist.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_list_job_token_group_allowlist, gitlab_add_group_job_token_allowlist

target_group_id.description: ID of the group to remove from the allowlist,required

name: gitlab_remove_project_job_token_allowlist
description:
Remove a project from the CI/CD job token inbound allowlist.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_list_job_token_inbound_allowlist, gitlab_add_project_job_token_allowlist

target_project_id.description: ID of the project to remove from the allowlist,required

name: gitlab_remove_secure_file
description:
Remove a CI/CD secure file.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_list_secure_files, gitlab_create_secure_file

name: gitlab_render_markdown
description:
Render arbitrary markdown text to HTML using the GitLab API. Supports GitLab Flavored Markdown (GFM) and project-scoped references.

Returns: JSON with the rendered HTML output.

See also: gitlab_wiki_create.

project.description: Project path for resolving references (e.g. group/project)

name: gitlab_repair_geo_site
description:
Repair the OAuth authentication of a Geo site.

Returns: JSON with the repaired Geo site configuration.

See also: gitlab_get_geo_site

id.description: Numeric ID of the Geo site,required

name: gitlab_repository_archive
description:
Get the download URL for a repository archive. Supports tar.gz, tar.bz2, zip formats and optional SHA/branch/tag/path filters. Returns the URL (does not download binary content).

Returns: archive download URL. See also: gitlab_project_get.

format.description: Archive format: tar.gz, tar.bz2, tbz, tbz2, tb2, bz2, tar, zip (default: tar.gz)

path.description: Subdirectory path to archive (omit for entire repo)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch, or tag to archive (default: default branch)

name: gitlab_repository_blob
description:
Get a git blob by SHA from a repository. For text content returns decoded text; for images returns viewable image data; for binary files returns metadata only. Requires a blob SHA obtained from gitlab_repository_tree. For reading files by path, use gitlab_file_get.

Returns: blob content (text/image) or metadata (binary). See also: gitlab_repository_tree.

sha.description: Blob SHA (from tree listing or commit diff),required

name: gitlab_repository_changelog_add
description:
Add changelog data to a changelog file by creating a commit. Requires version string. Optionally specify branch, from/to range, config file, and commit message.

Returns: JSON with the commit details for the changelog update. See also: gitlab_release_create.

branch.description: Branch to commit the changelog to (default: default branch)

config_file.description: Path to the changelog config file in the project

file.description: Path to the changelog file (default: CHANGELOG.md)

from.description: Start of the range (commit SHA or tag)

message.description: Commit message for the changelog update

to.description: End of the range (commit SHA or tag, default: HEAD)

trailer.description: Git trailer to use for changelog generation (default: Changelog)

name: gitlab_repository_changelog_generate
description:
Generate changelog data (notes) without committing. Returns the changelog notes as Markdown text. Requires version string.

Returns: Markdown changelog notes for the specified version.

See also: gitlab_repository_changelog_data.

config_file.description: Path to the changelog config file

from.description: Start of the range (commit SHA or tag)

to.description: End of the range (commit SHA or tag, default: HEAD)

name: gitlab_repository_compare
description:
Compare two branches, tags, or commits in a GitLab repository. Returns the list of commits between them and the diffs (changed files) with old/new paths and diff text.

Returns: JSON with commits and file diffs between the two refs. See also: gitlab_commit_diff, gitlab_branch_list.

from.description: Branch name, tag, or commit SHA to compare from,required

straight.description: Use straight comparison (from..to) instead of merge-base (from...to)

to.description: Branch name, tag, or commit SHA to compare to,required

unidiff.description: Return diffs in unified diff format

name: gitlab_repository_contributors
description:
List repository contributors with commit, addition, and deletion counts. Supports ordering by name, email, or commits and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of contributors with commit statistics and pagination. See also: gitlab_project_get.

order_by.description: Order by: name, email, or commits (default: commits)

sort.description: Sort direction: asc or desc (default: asc)

name: gitlab_repository_merge_base
description:
Find the common ancestor (merge base) commit of two or more branches, tags, or commits.

Returns: JSON with the merge base commit details.

See also: gitlab_repository_compare, gitlab_repository_tree.

refs.description: Two or more branch names, tags, or commit SHAs to find the merge base of,required

name: gitlab_repository_raw_blob
description:
Get the content of a git blob by SHA. For text content returns decoded text; for images returns viewable image data; for binary files returns metadata only. Requires a blob SHA from gitlab_repository_tree. For reading files by path, use gitlab_file_get instead.

Returns: blob content (text/image) or metadata (binary). See also: gitlab_repository_blob.

sha.description: Blob SHA (from tree listing or commit diff),required

name: gitlab_repository_tree
description:
List the files and directories (tree) of a GitLab repository at a given path and ref. 'ref' accepts a branch name, tag name, or commit SHA (defaults to the project's default branch if omitted). Returns file name, type (blob/tree), mode, and path with pagination. Use recursive=true to list all files in subdirectories. For reading file content, use gitlab_file_get instead.

Returns: JSON array of repository tree entries with pagination. See also: gitlab_file_get, gitlab_file_list.

path.description: Path inside the repository to list (default: root)

ref.description: Branch name, tag, or commit SHA (default: default branch)

name: gitlab_resolve_vulnerability
description: Resolve a vulnerability (requires Ultimate/Premium). Changes state to RESOLVED. Returns: updated vulnerability state. See also: gitlab_get_vulnerability, gitlab_confirm_vulnerability, gitlab_revert_vulnerability.

name: gitlab_retrieve_all_group_storage_moves
description:
Retrieve all group repository storage moves (admin only).

Returns: JSON with array of storage moves and pagination.

See also: gitlab_retrieve_group_storage_moves, gitlab_get_group_storage_move

name: gitlab_retrieve_all_project_storage_moves
description:
Retrieve all project repository storage moves (admin only).

Returns: JSON with array of storage moves and pagination.

See also: gitlab_retrieve_project_storage_moves, gitlab_get_project_storage_move

name: gitlab_retrieve_all_snippet_storage_moves
description:
Retrieve all snippet repository storage moves (admin only).

Returns: JSON with array of storage moves and pagination.

See also: gitlab_retrieve_snippet_storage_moves, gitlab_get_snippet_storage_move

name: gitlab_retrieve_group_storage_moves
description:
Retrieve all repository storage moves for a specific group (admin only).

Returns: JSON with array of storage moves and pagination.

See also: gitlab_retrieve_all_group_storage_moves

name: gitlab_retrieve_project_storage_moves
description:
Retrieve all repository storage moves for a specific project (admin only).

Returns: JSON with array of storage moves and pagination.

See also: gitlab_retrieve_all_project_storage_moves

name: gitlab_retrieve_snippet_storage_moves
description:
Retrieve all repository storage moves for a specific snippet (admin only).

Returns: JSON with array of storage moves and pagination.

See also: gitlab_retrieve_all_snippet_storage_moves

name: gitlab_retry_failed_external_status_check_for_project_mr
description:
Retry a failed external status check for a project merge request. Requires project_id, mr_iid, and check_id.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_list_project_mr_external_status_checks.

check_id.description: External status check ID to retry,required

name: gitlab_revert_vulnerability
description: Revert a vulnerability to detected state (requires Ultimate/Premium). Changes state back to DETECTED. Returns: updated vulnerability state. See also: gitlab_get_vulnerability, gitlab_dismiss_vulnerability, gitlab_resolve_vulnerability.

name: gitlab_review_mr_security
description:
Perform a security-focused review of a GitLab merge request using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Fetches MR details and code diffs, then requests LLM to identify injection vulnerabilities, auth issues, exposed secrets, and OWASP Top 10 findings. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown security review with vulnerability findings and OWASP Top 10 assessment.

See also: gitlab_analyze_mr_changes, gitlab_get_merge_request

name: gitlab_revoke_cluster_agent_token
description:
Revoke a cluster agent token

See also: gitlab_list_cluster_agent_tokens, gitlab_create_cluster_agent_token

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_revoke_group_personal_access_token
description:
Revoke a personal access token managed by a GitLab group.

Returns: JSON with revocation confirmation. See also: gitlab_list_group_personal_access_tokens.

name: gitlab_revoke_impersonation_token
description:
Revoke an impersonation token for a GitLab user (admin only).

See also: gitlab_list_impersonation_tokens, gitlab_create_impersonation_token

Returns: JSON with revocation confirmation.

name: gitlab_runner_controller_create
description:
Register a new runner controller. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with the created runner controller details.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_list, gitlab_runner_controller_scope_add_instance

name: gitlab_runner_controller_delete
description:
Delete a runner controller. Admin only. This action cannot be undone. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON confirmation of deletion.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_list, gitlab_runner_controller_create

name: gitlab_runner_controller_get
description:
Get detailed information about a runner controller. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with runner controller details (ID, description, state).

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_list, gitlab_runner_controller_token_list

name: gitlab_runner_controller_list
description:
List all runner controllers. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with array of runner controllers and pagination info.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_get, gitlab_runner_controller_scope_list

name: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_add_instance
description:
Add an instance-level scope to a runner controller. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with the added instance-level scope details.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_list, gitlab_runner_controller_scope_remove_instance

name: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_add_runner
description:
Add a runner scope to a runner controller. The runner must be instance-level. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with the added runner scope details.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_list, gitlab_runner_controller_scope_remove_runner

name: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_list
description:
List all scopes for a runner controller. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with array of scopes for the controller.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_list, gitlab_runner_controller_token_list

name: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_remove_instance
description:
Remove the instance-level scope from a runner controller. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON confirmation of scope removal.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_list, gitlab_runner_controller_scope_add_instance

name: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_remove_runner
description:
Remove a runner scope from a runner controller. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON confirmation of scope removal.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_scope_list, gitlab_runner_controller_scope_add_runner

runner_id.description: Runner ID to remove from scope,required

name: gitlab_runner_controller_token_create
description:
Create a new runner controller token. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with created token including ID and token value.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_token_list, gitlab_runner_controller_token_revoke

name: gitlab_runner_controller_token_get
description:
Get a specific runner controller token. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with token details including ID, description, and status.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_token_list, gitlab_runner_controller_token_rotate

name: gitlab_runner_controller_token_list
description:
List all tokens for a runner controller. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with tokens array including ID, description, and status.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_token_create, gitlab_runner_controller_list

name: gitlab_runner_controller_token_revoke
description:
Revoke a runner controller token. This action cannot be undone. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with deletion confirmation.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_token_list, gitlab_runner_controller_token_create

name: gitlab_runner_controller_token_rotate
description:
Rotate a runner controller token. Returns a new token replacing the old one. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with new token including ID and rotated token value.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_token_get, gitlab_runner_controller_token_revoke

name: gitlab_runner_controller_update
description:
Update a runner controller's description or state. Admin only. Experimental: may change or be removed.

Returns: JSON with the updated runner controller details.

See also: gitlab_runner_controller_get, gitlab_runner_controller_delete

state.description: New state: enabled, disabled, or dry_run

name: gitlab_runner_delete_by_token
description:
Delete a registered CI/CD runner using its authentication token. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_runner_delete_registered, gitlab_runner_verify

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_runner_delete_registered
description:
Delete a registered CI/CD runner by its ID. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_runner_register, gitlab_runner_list

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_runner_disable_project
description:
Remove a CI/CD runner from a project. The runner itself is not deleted.

See also: gitlab_runner_enable_project, gitlab_runner_list_project

Returns: confirmation message.

runner_id.description: Runner ID to remove from project,required

name: gitlab_runner_enable_project
description:
Assign an existing CI/CD runner to a project. Requires project_id and runner_id.

See also: gitlab_runner_disable_project, gitlab_runner_list_project

Returns: JSON with the runner assignment details.

name: gitlab_runner_get
description:
Get detailed information about a specific CI/CD runner by its ID. Returns description, status, tags, access level, projects, and groups.

See also: gitlab_runner_list, gitlab_runner_jobs

Returns: JSON with runner details including id, description, status, architecture, and platform.

name: gitlab_runner_jobs
description:
List jobs processed by a specific CI/CD runner. Filter by status (running, success, failed, canceled). Supports sorting and pagination.

See also: gitlab_runner_get, gitlab_runner_list

Returns: JSON array of jobs run by the runner with pagination.

order_by.description: Order by field: id (default)

status.description: Job status filter: running, success, failed, canceled

name: gitlab_runner_list
description:
List owned CI/CD runners. Filter by type (instance_type, group_type, project_type), status (online, offline, stale, never_contacted), paused state, and tags.

See also: gitlab_runner_get, gitlab_runner_list_project

Returns: JSON array of runners with pagination. Fields include id, description, status, and runner_type.

status.description: Runner status filter: online, offline, stale, never_contacted

tag_list.description: Comma-separated list of tags to filter by

type.description: Runner type filter: instance_type, group_type, project_type

name: gitlab_runner_list_all
description:
List all CI/CD runners in the GitLab instance (admin). Filter by type, status, paused state, and tags.

See also: gitlab_runner_list, gitlab_runner_list_project

Returns: JSON array of runners with pagination. Fields include id, description, status, and runner_type.

status.description: Runner status filter: online, offline, stale, never_contacted

tag_list.description: Comma-separated list of tags to filter by

type.description: Runner type filter: instance_type, group_type, project_type

name: gitlab_runner_list_group
description:
List CI/CD runners available in a specific group. Filter by type, status, and tags.

See also: gitlab_runner_list_project, gitlab_runner_list

Returns: JSON array of runners with pagination. Fields include id, description, status, and runner_type.

status.description: Runner status filter: online, offline, stale, never_contacted

tag_list.description: Comma-separated list of tags to filter by

type.description: Runner type filter: instance_type, group_type, project_type

name: gitlab_runner_list_managers
description:
List all managers (executors) for a specific CI/CD runner. Returns system ID, version, platform, architecture, IP address, and status.

See also: gitlab_runner_get, gitlab_runner_list

Returns: JSON array of runner managers.

name: gitlab_runner_list_project
description:
List CI/CD runners available in a specific project. Filter by type, status, and tags.

See also: gitlab_runner_enable_project, gitlab_runner_list_group

Returns: JSON array of runners with pagination. Fields include id, description, status, and runner_type.

status.description: Runner status filter: online, offline, stale, never_contacted

tag_list.description: Comma-separated list of tags to filter by

type.description: Runner type filter: instance_type, group_type, project_type

name: gitlab_runner_register
description:
Register a new CI/CD runner with a registration token. Optionally set description, tags, access level, and timeout.

See also: gitlab_runner_list, gitlab_runner_delete_registered

Returns: JSON with the registered runner details including token.

name: gitlab_runner_remove
description:
Remove a CI/CD runner by its ID. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_runner_list, gitlab_runner_delete_registered

Returns: confirmation message.

name: gitlab_runner_reset_group_reg_token
description:
Reset a group's runner registration token. Deprecated: scheduled for removal in GitLab 20.0.

See also: gitlab_runner_reset_instance_reg_token, gitlab_runner_reset_project_reg_token

Returns: JSON with the new registration token.

name: gitlab_runner_reset_instance_reg_token
description:
Reset the instance-level runner registration token. Deprecated: scheduled for removal in GitLab 20.0.

See also: gitlab_runner_reset_group_reg_token, gitlab_runner_reset_project_reg_token

Returns: JSON with the new registration token.

name: gitlab_runner_reset_project_reg_token
description:
Reset a project's runner registration token. Deprecated: scheduled for removal in GitLab 20.0.

See also: gitlab_runner_reset_instance_reg_token, gitlab_runner_reset_group_reg_token

Returns: JSON with the new registration token.

name: gitlab_runner_reset_token
description:
Reset the authentication token for a CI/CD runner. Returns the new token and expiry.

See also: gitlab_runner_verify, gitlab_runner_get

Returns: JSON with the new authentication token and expiry.

name: gitlab_runner_update
description:
Update a CI/CD runner's configuration. Modify description, paused state, tags, access level, maximum timeout, and maintenance note.

See also: gitlab_runner_get, gitlab_runner_list

Returns: JSON with the updated runner details.

locked.description: Whether runner is locked to current project

name: gitlab_runner_verify
description:
Verify a CI/CD runner authentication token. Returns success if the token is valid.

See also: gitlab_runner_reset_token, gitlab_runner_register

Returns: confirmation that the runner token is valid.

name: gitlab_schedule_all_group_storage_moves
description:
Schedule repository storage moves for all groups on a storage shard (admin only).

Returns: JSON with confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_schedule_group_storage_move

destination_storage_name.description: Name of the destination storage shard

source_storage_name.description: Name of the source storage shard

name: gitlab_schedule_all_project_storage_moves
description:
Schedule repository storage moves for all projects on a storage shard (admin only).

Returns: JSON with confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_schedule_project_storage_move

destination_storage_name.description: Name of the destination storage shard

source_storage_name.description: Name of the source storage shard

name: gitlab_schedule_all_snippet_storage_moves
description:
Schedule repository storage moves for all snippets on a storage shard (admin only).

Returns: JSON with confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_schedule_snippet_storage_move

destination_storage_name.description: Name of the destination storage shard

source_storage_name.description: Name of the source storage shard

name: gitlab_schedule_group_export
description:
Schedule an asynchronous export of a group.

Returns: JSON with the export schedule confirmation.

See also: gitlab_download_group_export, gitlab_schedule_project_export

name: gitlab_schedule_group_relations_export
description:
Schedule a new group relations export.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_list_group_relations_export_status, gitlab_schedule_group_export

group_id.description: The ID or URL-encoded path of the group,required

name: gitlab_schedule_group_storage_move
description:
Schedule a repository storage move for a group (admin only).

Returns: JSON with the scheduled storage move.

See also: gitlab_schedule_all_group_storage_moves

destination_storage_name.description: Name of the destination storage shard

name: gitlab_schedule_project_export
description:
Schedule an asynchronous export of a project. After scheduling, use gitlab_get_project_export_status to check progress.

Returns: JSON confirmation that the export was scheduled.

See also: gitlab_get_project_export_status, gitlab_schedule_group_export

description.description: Override the project description in the export

upload_http_method.description: HTTP method to use for the upload (PUT or POST)

upload_url.description: URL to upload the exported project to after export completes

name: gitlab_schedule_project_storage_move
description:
Schedule a repository storage move for a project (admin only).

Returns: JSON with the scheduled storage move.

See also: gitlab_schedule_all_project_storage_moves

destination_storage_name.description: Name of the destination storage shard

name: gitlab_schedule_snippet_storage_move
description:
Schedule a repository storage move for a snippet (admin only).

Returns: JSON with the scheduled storage move.

See also: gitlab_schedule_all_snippet_storage_moves

destination_storage_name.description: Name of the destination storage shard

name: gitlab_search_code
description:
Search for code (blobs) in GitLab. Scope is determined by which ID you provide: set project_id for project scope, group_id for group scope, or neither for global scope. Only one scope at a time. Returns matching file name, path, ref, and a content snippet with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching code blobs with pagination. See also: gitlab_file_get, gitlab_repository_tree.

ref.description: Branch or tag name to search in (default: default branch)

name: gitlab_search_commits
description:
Search for commits by keyword. Searches within a project (project_id), a group (group_id), or globally. Returns matching commits with ID, title, author, date, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching commits with pagination. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

name: gitlab_search_issues
description:
Search for issues by keyword. Searches within a project (project_id), a group (group_id), or globally. Returns matching issues with title, state, labels, assignees, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching issues with pagination. See also: gitlab_issue_get, gitlab_issue_list.

name: gitlab_search_merge_requests
description:
Search for merge requests by keyword. Searches within a project (project_id), a group (group_id), or globally. Returns matching merge requests with title, state, author, labels, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching merge requests with pagination. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_list.

name: gitlab_search_milestones
description:
Search for milestones by keyword. Searches within a project (project_id), a group (group_id), or globally. Returns matching milestones with title, state, dates, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching milestones with pagination. See also: gitlab_milestone_get.

name: gitlab_search_notes
description:
Search for notes (comments) within a GitLab project by keyword. Returns matching notes with body, author, notable type/ID, and timestamps with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching notes with pagination. See also: gitlab_issue_note_list, gitlab_mr_notes_list.

name: gitlab_search_projects
description:
Search for projects by keyword. Searches within a group (group_id) or globally. Returns matching projects with name, path, visibility, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching projects with pagination. See also: gitlab_project_get, gitlab_project_list.

group_id.description: Group ID or URL-encoded path (optional — omit for global search)

name: gitlab_search_snippets
description:
Search for snippet titles globally in GitLab. Returns matching snippets with title, file name, description, author, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching snippets with pagination. See also: gitlab_snippet_get.

name: gitlab_search_users
description:
Search for users by keyword. Searches within a project (project_id), a group (group_id), or globally. Returns matching users with username, name, state, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching users with pagination. See also: gitlab_get_user.

name: gitlab_search_wiki
description:
Search for wiki blobs by keyword. Searches within a project (project_id), a group (group_id), or globally. Returns matching wiki pages with title, slug, content, and format with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching wiki pages with pagination. See also: gitlab_wiki_get.

name: gitlab_server_status
description:
Check MCP server health and GitLab connectivity. Returns server version, author, department, repository, GitLab version, authentication status, current user, and response time. Use this to diagnose connection issues.

Returns: JSON with server health and connectivity information.

See also: gitlab_server_check_update, gitlab_get_metadata

name: gitlab_set_custom_attribute
description:
Set (create/update) a custom attribute for a user, group, or project (admin). Params: resource_type (required), resource_id (required), key (required), value (required).

Returns: JSON with the custom attribute details.

See also: gitlab_list_custom_attributes, gitlab_delete_custom_attribute

resource_type.description: Resource type: user, group, or project,required

name: gitlab_set_feature_flag
description:
Set or create a feature flag (admin). Requires name and value. Supports scoping to user, group, project, namespace, or repository.

Returns: JSON with the feature flag details.

See also: gitlab_list_features.

force.description: Force the change even if the flag is read-only

value.description: Value to set (true, false, integer percentage, or string),required

name: gitlab_set_jira_integration
description:
Configure the Jira integration for a project. Sets up the connection to a Jira instance with URL, credentials, and event triggers.

Returns: JSON with the configured Jira integration details.

See also: gitlab_list_integrations, gitlab_get_integration

comment_on_event_enabled.description: Add comments on Jira issues for events

jira_issue_regex.description: Custom regex for Jira issue keys

name: gitlab_set_project_mr_external_status_check_status
description:
Set the status of an external status check for a project merge request. Requires project_id, mr_iid, sha, external_status_check_id, and status.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_list_project_mr_external_status_checks.

sha.description: Head SHA of the merge request source branch,required

name: gitlab_set_user_status
description:
Set the status of the currently authenticated GitLab user. Supports setting emoji, message, availability (not_set/busy), and auto-clear duration.

See also: gitlab_get_user_status, gitlab_user_current

Returns: JSON with the updated user status.

clear_status_after.description: Duration after which to clear status: 30_minutes | 3_hours | 8_hours | 1_day | 3_days | 7_days | 30_days

emoji.description: The emoji to set for the status (e.g. coffee or speech_balloon)

name: gitlab_show_secure_file
description:
Show details of a CI/CD secure file.

Returns: JSON with secure file details.

See also: gitlab_list_secure_files, gitlab_create_secure_file

name: gitlab_snippet_content
description:
Get the raw content of a personal snippet.

Returns: raw snippet content. See also: gitlab_snippet_get.

name: gitlab_snippet_create
description:
Create a new personal snippet. Use 'files' for multi-file snippets or 'file_name'+'content' for single-file.

Returns: JSON with the created snippet details. See also: gitlab_snippet_get.

file_name.description: File name (single-file snippet, deprecated in favor of files)

files.description: Files to include in the snippet

file_path.description: File path for the snippet file,required

name: gitlab_snippet_delete
description:
Delete a personal snippet. This action cannot be undone.

name: gitlab_snippet_emoji_create
description:
Add an award emoji reaction to a project snippet.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_snippet_emoji_delete
description:
Delete an award emoji from a project snippet.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_snippet_emoji_get
description:
Get a single award emoji on a project snippet.

Returns: JSON with award emoji details including id, name, user, and created_at.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_snippet_emoji_list
description:
List all award emoji on a project snippet.

See also: gitlab_snippet_emoji_create, gitlab_issue_emoji_list

Returns: JSON array of award emoji with pagination. Fields include id, name, user, and awardable_type.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)

name: gitlab_snippet_explore
description:
List all public snippets on the GitLab instance.

Returns: JSON array of public snippets with pagination. See also: gitlab_snippet_get, gitlab_snippet_list.

name: gitlab_snippet_file_content
description:
Get the raw content of a specific file in a snippet by ref and filename.

Returns: raw snippet file content. See also: gitlab_snippet_get.

ref.description: Git ref (branch, tag, or commit SHA),required

name: gitlab_snippet_get
description:
Get a single personal snippet by ID.

Returns: JSON with snippet details. See also: gitlab_snippet_update.

name: gitlab_snippet_list
description:
List all snippets for the current authenticated user.

Returns: JSON array of snippets with pagination. See also: gitlab_snippet_get, gitlab_snippet_create.

name: gitlab_snippet_list_all
description:
List all snippets across the GitLab instance (admin endpoint).

Returns: JSON array of snippets with pagination. See also: gitlab_snippet_get, gitlab_snippet_create.

name: gitlab_snippet_note_create
description:
Add a comment (note) to a GitLab project snippet. Supports Markdown formatting.

Returns: JSON with created note including ID, body, author, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_project_snippet_get.

name: gitlab_snippet_note_delete
description:
Permanently delete a comment from a GitLab project snippet. Only the note author or a project maintainer can delete a note.

Returns: JSON with deletion confirmation. See also: gitlab_snippet_note_get.

note_id.description: ID of the note to delete,required

name: gitlab_snippet_note_emoji_create
description:
Add an award emoji reaction to a project snippet note.

Returns: JSON with the created award emoji.

See also: gitlab_snippet_note_emoji_list.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_snippet_note_emoji_delete
description:
Delete an award emoji from a project snippet note.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_snippet_note_emoji_list.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_snippet_note_emoji_get
description:
Get a single award emoji on a project snippet note.

Returns: JSON with award emoji details including id, name, user, and created_at.

See also: gitlab_snippet_note_emoji_list.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

name: gitlab_snippet_note_emoji_list
description:
List all award emoji on a project snippet note.

Returns: JSON array of award emoji with pagination. Fields include id, name, user, and awardable_type.

See also: gitlab_snippet_note_emoji_create.

iid.description: Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)

name: gitlab_snippet_note_get
description:
Get a single comment (note) from a GitLab project snippet by its note ID, including author, timestamps, body, and system flag.

Returns: JSON with note details including ID, body, author, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_snippet_note_list.

note_id.description: ID of the note to retrieve,required

name: gitlab_snippet_note_list
description:
List all comments (notes) on a GitLab project snippet. Supports ordering by created_at or updated_at, sort direction, and pagination.

Returns: JSON with notes array including body, author, timestamps, and system flags. See also: gitlab_project_snippet_get.

order_by.description: Order by field (created_at, updated_at)

name: gitlab_snippet_note_update
description:
Edit the body text of an existing comment on a GitLab project snippet. Only the note author or a project maintainer can update a note.

Returns: JSON with updated note including ID, body, author, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_snippet_note_get.

note_id.description: ID of the note to update,required

name: gitlab_snippet_update
description:
Update an existing personal snippet.

Returns: JSON with the updated snippet details. See also: gitlab_snippet_get.

content.description: New content (single-file, deprecated in favor of files)

file_name.description: New file name (single-file, deprecated in favor of files)

action.description: File action: create, update, delete, move,required

file_path.description: File path,required

visibility.description: New visibility: private, internal, or public

name: gitlab_start_bulk_import
description:
Start a new group or project bulk import migration (admin). Requires source GitLab URL, access token, and entities to migrate.

Returns: JSON with the migration details.

See also: gitlab_import_from_github, gitlab_schedule_group_export

access_token.description: Personal access token for source instance,required

source_full_path.description: Full path of the source entity

name: gitlab_subgroups_list
description:
List descendant subgroups of a GitLab group. Returns each subgroup's name, path, full path, description, visibility, and parent ID. Supports search filter and pagination.

Returns: paginated list of subgroups with id, name, path, full_path, visibility, and parent_id. See also: gitlab_group_get, gitlab_group_list.

all_available.description: Show all subgroups accessible by the authenticated user

order_by.description: Order subgroups by field (name, path, id, similarity)

owned.description: Limit to subgroups explicitly owned by the authenticated user

search.description: Filter subgroups by name or path

name: gitlab_summarize_issue
description:
Summarize a GitLab issue discussion using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Fetches issue details and all notes, then requests LLM summary of key decisions and action items. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown summary of the issue with key decisions and action items.

See also: gitlab_analyze_issue_scope, gitlab_list_issues

name: gitlab_summarize_mr_review
description:
Summarize a GitLab merge request review using LLM-assisted analysis via MCP sampling. Fetches MR details, discussions, and approval state, then requests LLM summary of reviewer feedback, unresolved threads, and action items. Requires the MCP client to support the sampling capability (human-in-the-loop approval).

Returns: Markdown summary of reviewer feedback, unresolved threads, and action items.

See also: gitlab_analyze_mr_changes, gitlab_list_mr_discussions

name: gitlab_tag_create
description: Create a Git tag in a GitLab project pointing to a ref (branch, tag, or SHA). Optionally include an annotation message for an annotated tag. Returns: tag name, target, message, protected status, and commit SHA. See also: gitlab_release_create, gitlab_tag_list.

message.description: Creates an annotated tag with this message

ref.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or another tag to create the tag from,required

name: gitlab_tag_delete
description:
Delete a Git tag from a GitLab project. If a release is associated with the tag, the release is also removed.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_tag_list, gitlab_tag_create.

tag_name.description: Name of the tag to delete,required

name: gitlab_tag_get
description:
Retrieve detailed information about a single Git tag. Returns tag name, target commit SHA, annotation message, and protection status.

Returns: name, target, message, protected status, and commit details. See also: gitlab_tag_create, gitlab_release_create.

name: gitlab_tag_get_protected
description: Get a single protected tag by name. Returns: tag name and create access levels (access_level, user_id, group_id per entry). See also: gitlab_tag_list_protected, gitlab_tag_protect.

name: gitlab_tag_get_signature
description:
Get the X.509 signature of a tag. Returns signature type, verification status, and certificate details.

Returns: signature_type, verification_status, and certificate details.

See also: gitlab_tag_get.

tag_name.description: Tag name to retrieve signature for,required

name: gitlab_tag_list
description:
List Git tags in a GitLab project. Supports search by name pattern, ordering by name/updated/version, and sort direction (asc/desc). Returns paginated results.

Returns: paginated list of tags with name, target SHA, message, and protected status. See also: gitlab_tag_get, gitlab_tag_create.

order_by.description: Order tags by field (name, updated)

search.description: Search query to filter tags by name

name: gitlab_tag_list_protected
description:
List protected tags in a GitLab project with their create access levels. Returns paginated results.

Returns: paginated list of protected tags with name and create access levels. See also: gitlab_tag_protect, gitlab_tag_get_protected.

name: gitlab_tag_protect
description: Protect a repository tag or wildcard pattern. Optionally set create access level or granular permissions (user, group, deploy key). Returns: protected tag name and create access levels. See also: gitlab_tag_unprotect, gitlab_tag_list_protected.

allowed_to_create.description: Granular create permissions (user_id, group_id, deploy_key_id, access_level)

deploy_key_id.description: Deploy key ID allowed to create

create_access_level.description: Access level allowed to create (0=No access, 30=Developer, 40=Maintainer)

name: gitlab_tag_unprotect
description:
Remove protection from a repository tag. The tag itself is not deleted.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_tag_protect, gitlab_tag_list_protected.

tag_name.description: Name of the protected tag to unprotect,required

name: gitlab_test_system_hook
description:
Test a system hook by ID (admin). Triggers a test event and returns the result.

Returns: JSON with test event result.

See also: gitlab_get_system_hook, gitlab_add_system_hook

name: gitlab_todo_list
description:
List pending to-do items for the authenticated user. Returns paginated results with action, target, type, and state. Use page and per_page for pagination.

Returns: JSON array of to-do items with pagination.

See also: gitlab_todo_mark_done, gitlab_todo_mark_all_done

action.description: Filter by action: assigned, mentioned, build_failed, marked, approval_required, directly_addressed

state.description: Filter by state: pending or done (default: pending)

type.description: Filter by target type: Issue, MergeRequest, DesignManagement::Design, AlertManagement::Alert

name: gitlab_todo_mark_all_done
description:
Mark ALL pending to-do items as done for the authenticated user. This affects all pending to-dos, not just those on a specific project.

Returns: JSON with count of marked to-do items.

See also: gitlab_todo_list

name: gitlab_todo_mark_done
description:
Mark a single pending to-do item as done by its ID. Use gitlab_todo_list to find to-do item IDs first.

Returns: JSON with the marked to-do item details.

See also: gitlab_todo_list

id.description: ID of the to-do item to mark as done,required

name: gitlab_track_event
description:
Track a single usage event. Params: event (required), send_to_snowplow, namespace_id, project_id.

Returns: JSON confirming the event was tracked.

See also: gitlab_track_events, gitlab_get_service_ping

send_to_snowplow.description: Whether to send event to Snowplow

name: gitlab_track_events
description:
Track multiple usage events in batch. Params: events (required, array of event objects).

Returns: JSON confirming the events were tracked.

See also: gitlab_track_event, gitlab_get_service_ping

send_to_snowplow.description: Whether to send event to Snowplow

name: gitlab_unban_user
description:
Unban a previously banned GitLab user (admin only).

See also: gitlab_ban_user, gitlab_unblock_user

Returns: JSON with action confirmation.

name: gitlab_unblock_user
description:
Unblock a previously blocked GitLab user, restoring login and API access (admin only).

See also: gitlab_block_user, gitlab_get_user

Returns: JSON with action confirmation.

name: gitlab_unlock_terraform_state
description:
Unlock a Terraform state

Returns: JSON confirming the state was unlocked.

See also: gitlab_lock_terraform_state, gitlab_get_terraform_state

name: gitlab_update_alert_metric_image
description:
Update a metric image for a GitLab alert.

Returns: JSON with the updated metric image details.

See also: gitlab_list_alert_metric_images, gitlab_upload_alert_metric_image

url.description: URL to link the metric image to

name: gitlab_update_appearance
description:
Update application appearance (title, description, messages, PWA settings). Requires admin access.

Returns: JSON with the updated appearance settings.

See also: gitlab_get_appearance, gitlab_create_broadcast_message

email_header_and_footer_enabled.description: Enable header and footer in emails

title.description: Application title displayed in the header

name: gitlab_update_broadcast_message
description:
Update a broadcast message. Requires admin access.

Returns: JSON with the updated broadcast message details.

See also: gitlab_get_broadcast_message, gitlab_delete_broadcast_message

target_path.description: Target path to show message on

name: gitlab_update_commit_discussion_note
description:
Update an existing note in a commit discussion thread.

Returns: JSON with updated note including ID, body, and author.

See also: gitlab_get_commit_discussion, gitlab_delete_commit_discussion_note

name: gitlab_update_compliance_policy_settings
description:
Update the admin-level compliance policy settings for the GitLab instance.

Returns: JSON with updated compliance policy settings.

See also: gitlab_get_compliance_policy_settings

csp_namespace_id.description: Namespace ID for the compliance security policy project

name: gitlab_update_epic_discussion_note
description:
Update an existing note in an epic discussion thread via the Work Items GraphQL API.

Returns: JSON with updated note including ID, body, and author.

See also: gitlab_get_epic_discussion, gitlab_delete_epic_discussion_note

full_path.description: Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required

iid.description: Epic IID within the group,required

name: gitlab_update_freeze_period
description:
Update a deploy freeze period's cron schedule or timezone.

Returns: JSON with updated freeze period including ID, cron schedule, and timezone.

See also: gitlab_get_freeze_period, gitlab_delete_freeze_period

name: gitlab_update_group_mr_approval_settings
description:
Update group-level merge request approval settings: control author/committer approval, approver list overrides, approval retention on push, and reauthentication. Only include settings you want to change.

Returns: JSON with all updated settings. Requires GitLab Premium.

See also: gitlab_get_group_mr_approval_settings.

allow_committer_approval.description: Allow committers to approve MRs they contributed to

allow_overrides_approver_list_per_mr.description: Allow overriding approver list per merge request

retain_approvals_on_push.description: Retain approvals when new commits are pushed

description:
Update a SCIM identity for a GitLab group. Changes the external UID.

name: gitlab_update_group_secret_push_protection
description:
Enable or disable secret push protection for a GitLab group. Optionally exclude specific projects.

Returns: JSON with updated group security settings. See also: gitlab_get_project_security_settings.

secret_push_protection_enabled.description: Enable or disable secret push protection,required

name: gitlab_update_issue_discussion_note
description:
Update an existing note in an issue discussion thread.

Returns: JSON with the updated note details. See also: gitlab_get_issue_discussion.

name: gitlab_update_package_protection_rule
description:
Update an existing package protection rule for a GitLab project.

Returns: JSON with updated rule details. See also: gitlab_list_package_protection_rules.

minimum_access_level_for_delete.description: Minimum access level for delete (maintainer, owner, admin)

minimum_access_level_for_push.description: Minimum access level for push (maintainer, owner, admin)

package_name_pattern.description: Package name pattern with optional wildcards

package_type.description: Package type (npm, pypi, maven, generic, etc.)

name: gitlab_update_project_external_status_check
description:
Update an external status check for a project. Requires project_id and check_id. Optionally update name, external_url, shared_secret, and protected_branch_ids.

Returns: updated status check with ID, name, external URL, HMAC, protected branches. See also: gitlab_list_project_external_status_checks.

check_id.description: External status check ID to update,required

shared_secret.description: Updated shared secret for HMAC verification

name: gitlab_update_project_mr_approval_settings
description:
Update project-level merge request approval settings: control author/committer approval, approver list overrides, approval retention on push, selective code owner removals, and reauthentication. Only include settings you want to change.

Returns: JSON with all updated settings. Requires GitLab Premium.

See also: gitlab_get_project_mr_approval_settings.

allow_author_approval.description: Allow merge request authors to approve their own MRs

allow_committer_approval.description: Allow committers to approve MRs they contributed to

allow_overrides_approver_list_per_mr.description: Allow overriding approver list per merge request

retain_approvals_on_push.description: Retain approvals when new commits are pushed

selective_code_owner_removals.description: Only remove Code Owner approvals for changed files (project-only)

name: gitlab_update_project_secret_push_protection
description:
Enable or disable secret push protection for a GitLab project.

secret_push_protection_enabled.description: Enable or disable secret push protection,required

name: gitlab_update_repository_submodule
description:
Update an existing submodule reference in a GitLab repository to point to a new commit SHA.

Returns: JSON with the updated submodule reference.

See also: gitlab_list_repository_submodules

branch.description: Branch name to commit the update to,required

commit_sha.description: Full commit SHA to update the submodule to,required

submodule.description: URL-encoded full path to the submodule,required

name: gitlab_update_settings
description:
Update application settings. Requires admin access. Pass settings as key-value map with snake_case keys matching GitLab API (e.g. signup_enabled, default_project_visibility).

Returns: JSON with the updated application settings.

See also: gitlab_get_settings.

settings.description: Map of setting_name to new value. Use snake_case keys matching GitLab API fields (e.g. signup_enabled, default_project_visibility, max_artifacts_size).,required

name: gitlab_update_snippet_discussion_note
description:
Update an existing note in a snippet discussion thread.

Returns: JSON with the updated note details.

See also: gitlab_get_snippet_discussion, gitlab_delete_snippet_discussion_note

name: gitlab_update_topic
description:
Update a project topic. Requires admin access.

See also: gitlab_get_topic, gitlab_delete_topic

Returns: JSON with the updated topic details.

name: gitlab_update_work_item
description:
Update an existing work item by IID. Supports changing title, state (CLOSE/REOPEN), description, assignees, milestone, labels (add/remove), dates, weight, health status, iteration, color, and status (TODO/IN_PROGRESS/DONE/WONT_DO/DUPLICATE). Experimental: the Work Items API may introduce breaking changes between minor versions.

Returns: JSON with the updated work item details.

See also: gitlab_get_work_item, gitlab_delete_work_item

add_label_ids.description: Global IDs of labels to add

assignee_ids.description: Global IDs of assignees (empty array to remove all)

crm_contact_ids.description: CRM contact IDs (empty array to remove all)

full_path.description: Full path of the project or group (e.g. my-group/my-project),required

iid.description: Work item IID,required

parent_id.description: Global ID of the parent work item

remove_label_ids.description: Global IDs of labels to remove

status.description: Work item status: TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE, WONT_DO, or DUPLICATE

name: gitlab_upload_alert_metric_image
description:
Upload a metric image for a GitLab alert. Provide either file_path (absolute path to a local image file) or content_base64 (base64-encoded content), not both.

Returns: JSON with the uploaded metric image details.

See also: gitlab_list_alert_metric_images, gitlab_update_alert_metric_image

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded image content. Only one of file_path or content_base64 should be provided.

file_path.description: Absolute path to a local image file on the MCP server filesystem. Alternative to content_base64. Only one of file_path or content_base64 should be provided.

url.description: URL to link the metric image to

name: gitlab_user_contribution_event_list
description:
List contribution events for the authenticated user. Supports filtering by action type, target type, date range, sort order, scope, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of contribution events with pagination.

See also: gitlab_project_event_list, gitlab_get_current_user

action.description: Filter by action type (created, updated, closed, reopened, pushed, commented, merged, joined, left, destroyed, expired)

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)

scope.description: Include events from all projects (all) or only user's projects

target_type.description: Filter by target type (issue, milestone, merge_request, note, project, snippet, user)

name: gitlab_user_current
description:
Retrieve information about the currently authenticated GitLab user. Returns user ID, username, name, email, state, avatar URL, web URL, and admin status. Useful for confirming identity and permissions.

See also: gitlab_get_user, gitlab_list_users

Returns: JSON with current user profile details.

name: gitlab_vulnerability_severity_count
description: Get vulnerability severity counts for a project (requires Ultimate/Premium). Returns: counts per severity level (critical, high, medium, low, info, unknown) and total. See also: gitlab_list_vulnerabilities, gitlab_pipeline_security_summary.

project_path.description: Full path of the project (e.g. my-group/my-project),required

name: gitlab_wiki_create
description:
Create a new wiki page in a GitLab project. Supports Markdown (default), RDoc, AsciiDoc, and Org formats.

Returns: JSON with created wiki page including title, slug, and content. See also: gitlab_wiki_get.

content.description: Content of the wiki page (Markdown, RDoc, AsciiDoc, or Org),required

format.description: Content format: markdown (default), rdoc, asciidoc, or org

name: gitlab_wiki_delete
description:
Delete a wiki page by slug. This action cannot be undone. Use gitlab_wiki_list to find available page slugs.

Returns: JSON with deletion confirmation. See also: gitlab_wiki_list.

slug.description: URL-encoded slug of the wiki page to delete,required

name: gitlab_wiki_get
description:
Get a single wiki page by slug. Set render_html=true to get HTML-rendered content. Set version to a commit SHA to retrieve a specific historical version of the page. Use gitlab_wiki_list to discover available page slugs.

Returns: JSON with wiki page content, title, slug, format, and encoding. See also: gitlab_wiki_update.

render_html.description: Return HTML-rendered content instead of raw format

slug.description: URL-encoded slug of the wiki page (e.g. 'my-page'),required

version.description: Wiki page version SHA to retrieve a specific revision

name: gitlab_wiki_list
description:
List all wiki pages in a GitLab project. Optionally include page content by setting with_content=true.

Returns: JSON with wiki pages array including title, slug, format, and encoding. See also: gitlab_wiki_get, gitlab_wiki_create.

name: gitlab_wiki_update
description:
Update an existing wiki page by slug. Can change the title, content, and format. At least one of title, content, or format must be provided.

Returns: JSON with updated wiki page including title, slug, and content. See also: gitlab_wiki_get.

content.description: New content for the wiki page

format.description: Content format: markdown, rdoc, asciidoc, or org

slug.description: URL-encoded slug of the wiki page to update,required

title.description: New title for the wiki page

name: gitlab_wiki_upload_attachment
description:
Upload a file attachment to a project wiki. Provide file content as base64 or a local file path.

Returns: JSON with file path and Markdown embed snippet. See also: gitlab_wiki_create.

branch.description: Branch to upload the attachment to

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded file content. Provide either content_base64 or file_path

file_path.description: Absolute path to a local file. Provide either file_path or content_base64

filename.description: Name of the file to upload (e.g. diagram.png),required
